detailed.name: anatomical entity
detailed.def: Biological entity that is either an individual member of a biological species or constitutes the structural organization of an individual member of a biological species.

detailed.name: ventral mesofurco-profurcal muscle
detailed.def: The profurcal muscle that arises from the mesofurcal bridge and inserts proximally of the profurca.

detailed.name: A1 flap
detailed.def: The carina that is located distally on the scape, that envelopes the proximal pedicel.

detailed.name: anatomical structure
detailed.def: Material anatomical entity which has inherent 3D shape and is generated by coordinated expression of the organism's own genome.

detailed.name: portion of organism substance
detailed.def: Material anatomical entity in a gaseous, liquid, semisolid or solid state; produced by anatomical structures or derived from inhaled and ingested substances that have been modified by anatomical structures as they pass through the body.

detailed.name: anatomical space
detailed.def: Non-material anatomical entity of three dimensions, that is generated by morphogenetic or other physiologic processes; is surrounded by one or more anatomical structures; contains one or more organism substances or anatomical structures.

detailed.name: material anatomical entity
detailed.def: Anatomical entity that has mass.

detailed.name: immaterial anatomical entity
detailed.def: Anatomical entity that has no mass.

detailed.name: anatomical line
detailed.def: Non-material anatomical entity of one dimension, which forms a boundary of an anatomical surface or is a modulation of an anatomical surface.

detailed.name: anatomical point
detailed.def: Non-material anatomical entity of zero dimension, which forms a boundary of an anatomical line or surface.

detailed.name: anatomical surface
detailed.def: Non-material anatomical entity of two dimensions, that is demarcated by anatomical lines or points on the external or internal surfaces of anatomical structures.

detailed.name: anatomical system
detailed.def: Anatomical group that is has as its parts distinct anatomical structures interconnected by anatomical structures at a lower level of granularity.

detailed.name: multi-cellular organism
detailed.def: Anatomical structure that is an individual member of a species and consists of more than one cell.

detailed.name: cell
detailed.def: Anatomical structure that has as its parts a maximally connected cell compartment surrounded by a plasma membrane.

detailed.name: cell component
detailed.def: Anatomical structure that is a direct part of the cell.

detailed.name: abdomen
detailed.def: The tagma that is located posterior to the thorax.

detailed.name: abdominal segment
detailed.def: The segment that is located posterior to the head and is does not bear legs.

detailed.name: abdominal segment 1
detailed.def: The abdominal segment that is located between the metathorax and abdominal segment 2.

detailed.name: abdominal segment 10
detailed.def: The abdominal segment that is located posterior to abdominal segment 9.

detailed.name: compound organ component
detailed.def: Multi-tissue structure that is part of a compound organ.

detailed.name: abdominal segment 2
detailed.def: The abdominal segment that has a sternum and is located posterior to the first abdominal spiracle.

synonyms.name: lateral muscle of abdominal segment II

detailed.name: simple organ
detailed.def: Multi-tissue structure that is not part of a compound organ.

detailed.name: abdominal segment 3
detailed.def: The abdominal segment that is located between abdominal segment 2 and abdominal segment 4.

detailed.name: abdominal segment 4
detailed.def: The abdominal segment that is located between abdominal segment 3 and abdominal segment 5.

detailed.name: compound organ
detailed.def: Anatomical structure that has as its parts two or more multi-tissue structures of at least two different types and which through specific morphogenetic processes forms a single distinct structural unit demarcated by bona fide boundaries from other distinct anatomical structures of different types.

detailed.name: abdominal segment 5
detailed.def: The abdominal segment that is located between abdominal segment 4 and abdominal segment 6.

detailed.name: abdominal segment 6
detailed.def: The abdominal segment that is located between abdominal segment 5 and abdominal segment 7.

detailed.name: male organism
detailed.def: Gonochoristic organism that can produce male gametes.

detailed.name: female organism
detailed.def: Gonochoristic organism that can produce female gametes.

detailed.name: hermaphroditic organism
detailed.def: Multi-cellular organism that can produce both male and female gametes.

detailed.name: asexual organism
detailed.def: Multi-cellular organism that does not produce gametes.

detailed.name: abdominal segment 7
detailed.def: The abdominal segment that is located between abdominal segment 6 and abdominal segment 8.

detailed.name: organism subdivision
detailed.def: Anatomical structure which is a primary subdivision of whole organism. The mereological sum of these is the whole organism.

detailed.name: abdominal segment 8
detailed.def: The abdominal segment that is located between abdominal segment 7 and abdominal segment 9.

detailed.name: abdominal segment 9
detailed.def: The abdominal segment that is located between abdominal segment 8 and abdominal segment 10.

detailed.name: abdominal sternum 2
detailed.def: The abdominal sternum that is located on abdominal segment 2.

detailed.name: abdominal sternum 3
detailed.def: The abdominal sternum that is located on abdominal segment 3.

detailed.name: abdominal sternum 4
detailed.def: The abdominal sternum that is located on abdominal segment 4.

detailed.name: abdominal sternum 5
detailed.def: The abdominal sternum that is located on abdominal segment 5.

detailed.name: abdominal sternum 6
detailed.def: The abdominal sternum that is located on abdominal segment 6.

detailed.name: acellular anatomical structure
detailed.def: Anatomical structure that consists of cell parts and cell substances and together does not constitute a cell or a tissue.

detailed.name: anatomical cluster
detailed.def: The anatomical group that has its parts adjacent to one another.

detailed.name: extraembryonic structure
detailed.def: Anatomical structure that is contiguous with the embryo and is comprised of portions of tissue or cells that will not contribute to the embryo.

detailed.name: portion of tissue
detailed.def: Anatomical structure, that consists of similar cells and intercellular matrix, aggregated according to genetically determined spatial relationships.

detailed.name: abdominal sternum 7
detailed.def: The abdominal sternum that is located on abdominal segment 7.

detailed.name: sequential hermaphroditic organism
detailed.def: Hermaphroditic organism that produces gametes first of one sex, and then later of the other sex.

detailed.name: synchronous hermaphroditic organism
detailed.def: Hermaphroditic organism that produces both male and female gametes at the same time.

detailed.name: abdominal sternum 9
detailed.def: The abdominal sternum that is located on abdominal segment 9.

detailed.name: gonochoristic organism
detailed.def: Multi-cellular organism that has male and female sexes.

detailed.name: protandrous hermaphroditic organism
detailed.def: Sequential hermaphroditic organism that produces gametes first of the male sex, and then later of the female sex.

detailed.name: protogynous hermaphroditic organism
detailed.def: Sequential hermaphroditic organism that produces gametes first of the female sex, and then later of the male sex.

detailed.name: abdominal tergum 1
detailed.def: The tergum that is located on abdominal segment 1.

detailed.name: abdominal tergum 10
detailed.def: The abdominal tergum that is located in the abdominal segment 10.

detailed.name: abdominal tergum 2
detailed.def: The tergum that is located on abdominal segment 2.

detailed.name: anatomical group
detailed.def: Anatomical structure consisting of at least two non-overlapping organs, multi-tissue aggregates or portion of tissues or cells of different types that does not constitute an organism, organ, multi-tissue aggregate, or portion of tissue.

detailed.name: multi-tissue structure
detailed.def: Anatomical structure that has as its parts two or more portions of tissue of at least two different types and which through specific morphogenetic processes forms a single distinct structural unit demarcated by bona-fide boundaries from other distinct structural units of different types.

detailed.name: abdominal tergum 3
detailed.def: The tergum that is located on the abdominal segment 3.

detailed.name: abdominal tergum 4
detailed.def: The tergum that is located on abdominal segment 4.

detailed.name: abdominal tergum 5
detailed.def: The tergum that is located on abdominal segment 5.

detailed.name: abdominal tergum 6
detailed.def: The tergum that is located on abdominal segment 6.

detailed.name: abdominal tergum 7
detailed.def: The tergum that is located on abdominal segment 7.

detailed.name: abdominal tergum 8
detailed.def: The tergum that is located on abdominal segment 8.

detailed.name: cell space
detailed.def: Anatomical space that is part of a cell.

detailed.name: portion of cell substance
detailed.def: Portion of organism substance located within a cell.

detailed.name: basal lamina
detailed.def: Acellular anatomical structure that consists of a thin sheet of fibrous proteins that underlie and support the cells of an epithelium. It separates the cells of an epithelium from any underlying tissue.

detailed.name: epithelium
detailed.def: Portion of tissue, that consists of one or more layers of epithelial cells connected to each other by cell junctions and which is underlain by a basal lamina.

detailed.name: simple cuboidal epithelium
detailed.def: Unilaminar epithelium that consists of a single layer of cuboidal cells.

detailed.name: simple columnar epithelium
detailed.def: Unilaminar epithelium that consists of a single layer of columnar cells.

detailed.name: multilaminar epithelium
detailed.def: Epithelium that consists of more than one layer of epithelial cells.

detailed.name: simple squamous epithelium
detailed.def: Unilaminar epithelium that consists of a single layer of squamous cells.

detailed.name: atypical epithelium
detailed.def: Epithelium that consists of epithelial cells not arranged in one ore more layers.

detailed.name: cavitated compound organ
detailed.def: Compound organ that contains one or more macroscopic anatomical spaces.

detailed.name: unilaminar epithelium
detailed.def: Epithelium that consists of a single layer of epithelial cells.

detailed.name: solid compound organ
detailed.def: Compound organ that does not contain macroscopic anatomical spaces.

detailed.name: T9
detailed.def: The tergite that is articulted with the first valvifer and is connected to the second valvifer via muscles.

detailed.name: abscissa
detailed.def: The wing vein that is delimited by its intersection with two other abscissae.

detailed.name: epithelial cell
detailed.def: Cell which has as its part a cytoskeleton that allows for tight cell to cell contact and which has apical-basal cell polarity.

detailed.name: accessory gland
detailed.def: The gland that empties into one of the reproductive ducts.

detailed.name: acetabular carina
detailed.def: The area that is located medially on the epicnemial carina.

detailed.name: acetabular groove
detailed.def: The impression that extends from the mandibular acetabulum to the preapical tooth.

detailed.name: acetabulum
detailed.def: The area that is concave and accommodates the base of a segment.

detailed.name: acropleural apodeme
detailed.def: The apodeme that is located anterodorsally on the mesopleuron and corresponds to the site of origin of the third mesopleuro-mesonotal muscle.

detailed.name: acropleural sulcus
detailed.def: The line that delimits posteroventrally the acropleuron.

detailed.name: acrosternite
detailed.def: The area that is located anteriorly of the antecosta of a sternum.

detailed.name: adult abdomen
detailed.def: The abdomen that is part of the adult.

detailed.name: adult tagma
detailed.def: The tagma that is part of the adult.

detailed.name: adult thorax
detailed.def: The thorax that is part of the adult.

detailed.name: aedeagus
detailed.def: The anatomical cluster that is composed of sclerites that are adjacent to the distal end of the ejaculatory duct.

detailed.name: alinotum
detailed.def: The notum that is connected to the wings.

detailed.name: anal vein
detailed.def: The wing vein that articulates proximally with the third axillary sclerite and is located just posteriorly of the claval fold.

detailed.name: anepisternum
detailed.def: The area of the episternum delimited by the paracoxal suture, basalar cleft and anapleural cleft.

detailed.name: annellus
detailed.def: The area that is located proximally on the first flagellomere and is less sclerotized and narrower than the distal part of the first flagellomere.

detailed.name: annulus
detailed.def: The sclerite that is ring-like and is not attached to muscles.

detailed.name: anteclypeus
detailed.def: The area that is distal and limited proximally by a sulcus.

detailed.name: antecosta
detailed.def: The ridge that extends along the anterior margin of the tergum and sternum and corresponds to the site of attachment of the median longitudinal intersegmental muscles.

detailed.name: antecostal sulcus
detailed.def: The sulcus that corresponds to the antecosta.

detailed.name: antemedian mesonotal wing process
detailed.def: The fossa that is located posteroventrally on the preaxilla immediately anterior to the transscutal line and articulates with the mesal condyle of the first axillary sclerite of the fore wing.

detailed.name: antenna
detailed.def: The appendage that is composed of ringlike sclerites and the anatomical structures encircled by these sclerites and that is articulated with the cranium.

detailed.name: antenna cleaner
detailed.def: The anatomical cluster that is composed of the probasitarsus and calcar.

detailed.name: antennal rim
detailed.def: The rim that surrounds the antennal foramen.

detailed.name: antennal segment
detailed.def: The antennomere that is an appendage segment.

detailed.name: antennal shelf
detailed.def: The area that is delimited by two transverse edges extending just ventrally and dorsally of the antennal foramina.

detailed.name: antennal suture
detailed.def: The sulcus that surrounds the antennal rim.

detailed.name: antennomere
detailed.def: The anatomical structure that is delimited by the proximal and distal margins of the antennal sclerite.

detailed.name: posterior cranio-mandibular muscle
detailed.def: The mandibular muscle that arises posterolaterally from the cranium and inserts on the tendon attached posteroproximal part of the mandible.

detailed.name: frontal carina
detailed.comment: Historically duplicated HAO in mx from pre-sensu model.

detailed.name: anterior extension of mesofurca
detailed.def: The area that is located distally on the lateral mesofurcal arm and corresponds to the site of origin of the lateral anterior mesopleuro-mesofurcal muscle.

detailed.name: anterior extension of the preaxilla
detailed.def: The area that is projected, located anteriorly on the preaxilla and fits into the dorsal incision of the pronotum.

detailed.name: anterior glossal sclerite
detailed.def: The sclerite of the anterior glossal plate that is lateral to the median conjunctiva of the anterior glossal plate.

detailed.name: anterior labral retractor
detailed.def: The labral muscle that is unpaired, arises proximally from the upper face and inserts on the median proximal margin of the labrum.

detailed.name: anterior margin of the speculum
detailed.def: The ridge that delimits the site of origin of the anterior mesopleuro-mesofurcal muscle.

detailed.name: anterior mesofurco-mesolaterophragmal muscle
detailed.def: The mesofurco-mesolaterophragmal muscle that originates from the mesofurca and inserts anteriorly on the mesolaterophragma.

detailed.name: anterior mesopleural area
detailed.def: The area that is limited posteriorly by the anterior mesopleural sulcus.

detailed.name: anterior mesopleural sulcus
detailed.def: The sulcus that extends between the lateral end of the acetabular carina and the dorsal margin of the mesopleuron anterior to the mesopleural wing articulation.

detailed.name: anterior mesopleuro-mesofurcal muscle
detailed.def: The mesofurcal muscle that originates from the mesopleuron and inserts distally on the mesofurca.

key: anterior mesopleuro-third axillary sclerite of fore wing muscle

detailed.name: anterior mesopleuro-third axillary sclerite of fore wing muscle
anterior notal wing process
detailed.def: The fossa that is located posterolaterally on the preaxilla and articulates with the anterior condyle of the first axillary sclerite of the fore wing.

key: anterior process of the mesofurcal bridge

detailed.name: anterior process of the mesofurcal bridge
detailed.def: The apodeme that is located medially on the mesofurcal bridge.

detailed.name: anterior process of the pronotum
detailed.def: The process that is located medially on the anterior rim of pronotum.

detailed.name: anterior process of the prosternum
detailed.def: The projection that is median and is located on the anterior margin of the basisternum.

detailed.name: anterior profurcal lamella
detailed.def: The ridge that extends along the median line of the dorsal profurcal lamella and that receives the site of attachment of the profurco-laterocervical muscle.

detailed.name: anterior rim of pronotum
detailed.def: The area that extends along the anterior margin of the pronotum and is delimited posteriorly by the transverse pronotal suclus.

detailed.name: anterior tentorial pit
detailed.def: The tentorial pit that corresponds to the anterior end of the tentorium and partially delimits the clypeus.

detailed.name: antero-dorsal incision of mesopleuron
detailed.def: The notch that is located anteriorly on the dorsal margin of the mesopleuron and accommodates the mesobasalare.

detailed.name: anteroadmedian line
detailed.def: The signum that is submedian and located on the anterior margin of the mesoscutum and corresponds to the site of origin of the longitudinal flight muscle.

detailed.name: anterolateral depression of the petiole
detailed.def: The depression that is located anterolaterally on the abdominal tergum 2 and accommodates the propodeal tooth.

detailed.name: anterolateral mesoscutal corner
detailed.def: The corner that is located anterolaterally on the mesoscutum and is adjacent to the dorsal pronotal corner.

detailed.name: anteromedian pronotal ridge
detailed.def: The ridge that extends parallel to the anterior (ventral) margin of the pronotum and delimits dorsally the site of origin of the pronoto-propleural arm muscles.

detailed.name: anteroventral pronotal inflection
detailed.def: The inflection that is located on the anteroventral margin of the pronotum.

detailed.name: antescutal depression
detailed.def: The area that is median, concave, triangular, is located on the posterior margin of the pronotum and is delimited by a carina anteriorly.

detailed.name: apex gonostipitis
detailed.def: The apodeme that is located medially on the proximoventral margin of the gonostipes and is the site of origin of the ventral gonostyle/volsella complex-penisvalval muscles.

detailed.name: apex of the calcar
detailed.def: The area that extends distally of the apex of the velum or comb of the spur.

detailed.name: apical clavomere ventral pit
detailed.def: The depression accommodates the sensilla that is located ventrally on the  apical flagellomere.

detailed.name: apical flagellomere
detailed.def: The flagellomere that is located distally on the flagellum.

detailed.name: apical parossiculal seta
detailed.def: The seta that is located close to the distoventral margin of the gonostyle/volsella complex just laterally of the gonostyle/volsella complex-gonossiculus articulation.

detailed.name: apical process of the labrum
detailed.def: The process that located distomedially on the labrum.

detailed.name: apical row of spines
detailed.def: The row of spurs that is located on the internal margin of the calcar distally of the velum.

detailed.name: apiceps
detailed.def: The area that is the distal part of the gonossiculus and is connected to the parossiculus via membranous conjunctiva.

detailed.name: apodeme
detailed.def: The process that is internal.

detailed.name: apophysis
detailed.def: The apodeme that is tubercle-like or spine-like.

detailed.name: appendage
detailed.def: The anatomical structure that is encircled by the evagination of the integument and whose integumental components are attached to the body via muscles.

detailed.name: appendage segment
detailed.comment: We are using segment for both leg, antenna and body divisions

detailed.name: area
detailed.def: The anatomical structure of the cuticle that is delimited by material or immaterial anatomical entities.

detailed.name: areolet
detailed.def: The wing cell of the fore wing that is minute, round and is delimited by the radio-medial cross veins.

detailed.name: arolium
detailed.def: The lobe that is connected proximodorsally with the manubrium and proximoventrally with the planta.

detailed.name: articular line
detailed.def: The anatomical line that extends on the border between two articulating sclerites.

detailed.name: articular process
detailed.def: The projection that bears the articular surface.

detailed.name: articulation
detailed.def: The anatomical cluster that is composed of two adjacent articular surfaces.

detailed.name: aulax
detailed.def: The impression that is on the first valvifer-first valvula complex accommodates the rhachis.

relationships.target_name: dorsal ramus of the first valvula

detailed.name: auxiliar sclerite
detailed.def: The sclerite that is located between the unguitractor and the bases of claws.

detailed.name: axilla
detailed.def: The area that is located posterior to the transscutal articulation and anterior to the scutoscutellar suture.

detailed.name: axillar carina
detailed.def: The line that separates the dorsal and lateral axillar surfaces.

detailed.name: axillary cord
detailed.def: The area that is heavily sclerotized and located proximally on the posterior wing margin.

detailed.name: axillary region
detailed.def: The anatomical cluster that is composed of the axillary sclerites.

detailed.name: axillary sclerite
detailed.def: The sclerite that is located on the axillary region and articulates with one of the thoracic wing processes.

detailed.name: axillula
detailed.def: The area that is located laterally on the mesoscutellum and is limited anteriorly by the scutoscutellar suture and medially by the axillular line.

detailed.name: axillular carina
detailed.def: The axillular line that is a carina.

detailed.name: axillular setae
detailed.def: The row of setae that extends along the median margin of axillula.

detailed.name: band
detailed.def: The area that is oblong and differs from surrounding areas in coloration, surface sculpture and setae.

detailed.name: bar
detailed.def: The process that is linear and wider proximally than distally.

detailed.name: basal bridge
detailed.def: The area that connects the parosicculi.

synonyms.name: sclerotic bridge uniting bases of volsellae

detailed.name: basal grooves
detailed.def: The row of impressions that extends along the anterior margin of an abdominal tergum or a sternum.

detailed.name: gonostipes/volsella complex
detailed.def: The anatomical cluster that is part of the gonostyle/volsella complex and is composed of the sclerites that are proximal to the harpe.

key: basal transverse carina of third abdominal tergite

detailed.name: basal transverse carina of third abdominal tergite
detailed.def: The carina that extends along the proximal margin of the third abdominal tergite.

detailed.name: basal vein
detailed.def: The wing vein that is basally located on the fore wing and connects or nearly connects the submarginal vein and the medial plus cubital veins.

detailed.name: basalare
detailed.def: The sclerite that is located on the dorsal margin of the pectus of pterothoracic segment anteriorly of the pleural wing articulation.

detailed.name: basicosta
detailed.def: The ridge that is located proximally and submarginally in the coxa.

detailed.name: basicostal suture
detailed.def: The sulcus that corresponds to the basicosta.

detailed.name: basicoxite
detailed.def: The area that is located on the coxa proximal to the basicostal suture.

detailed.name: basisternum
detailed.def: The area that is located anteriorly of the site of origin of the furca.

detailed.name: basitarsal notch
detailed.def: The notch that is located proximally on the median surface of the probasitarsus.

detailed.name: basitarsus
detailed.def: The tarsomere that is the proximal most annulus of the tarsus, connected proximally with the tibia and distally with the second tarsomere via membranous conjunctivae.

detailed.name: basiura
detailed.def: The area that is the proximal part of the gonossiculus and corresponds to the site of insertion of medial penisvalvo-gonossiculal muscle.

detailed.name: basivalvula
detailed.def: The anatomical cluster that is composed of small sclerites and is located proximally on the first valvulae.

detailed.name: blade
detailed.def: The area that is located distally on the mandible.

detailed.name: body
detailed.def: The anatomical cluster that is composed of the whole organism but which excludes the antennae, legs and wings.

detailed.name: body segment
detailed.def: The segment that is the part of the body.

detailed.name: bulla
detailed.def: The anatomical region of the wing vein that is less melanized and more flexible than surrounding anatomical wing vein regions and corresponds to the intersection of the wing vein with a flexion line.

detailed.name: calyx
detailed.def: The broadened portion of the lateral oviduct is enlarged and contains virus particles (characterized by bluish autofluorescence).

detailed.name: cardo
detailed.def: The sclerite that is articulated with the cranium at the cranio-cardinal articulation, is connected to the stipes distolaterally via the stipitocardinal hinge and receives the site of attachment of the occipito-cardinal muscle.

detailed.name: carina
detailed.def: The process that is elongate and external.

detailed.name: cenchrus
detailed.def: The conjunctiva that is folded and located on the anterior margin of the metanotum and covered (at least partially) with setae.

detailed.name: cercus
detailed.def: The sense organ that located apicolaterally on one of the apicalmost abdominal terga and attaches to a large nerve cord.

detailed.name: cervical apodeme
detailed.def: The apodeme that is located posteriorly of and continuous with the cervical prominence and corresponds to the site of insertion of laterocervical muscles.

detailed.name: cervical prominence
detailed.def: The articular process that is located on the anterior margin of the propleuron and bears the cranial condyle of the propleuron.

detailed.name: cervical pronotal area
detailed.def: The area that is located medially on the pronotum and is delimited laterally by the epomial carina.

detailed.name: cervical sclerite
detailed.comment: There is no cervical sclerite and hence cervix in Hymenoptera (cervical prominence is not an independent sclerite)

detailed.name: cervix
detailed.comment: There are no cervical sclerites in Hymenoptera.

detailed.name: chaetobothria
detailed.def: The row of stout spines that is located along the anterior edge of the protibia.

detailed.name: chalaza
detailed.def: The seta that is located on a process.

detailed.name: chamber of the metanotum
detailed.def: The anatomical space that is delimited dorsally by the wall of the metascutellum, anteriorly and posteriorly by the bifurcated internal metanotal ridge.

detailed.name: cheek
detailed.def: The area that is located ventrally in between the eye and the oral foramen.

detailed.name: cibarium
detailed.def: The anatomical space that is the anteriormost part of the alimentary canal and is delimited proximally by the proximomedian margin of the sitophore and distally by the functional mouth.

detailed.name: circumocular ridge
detailed.def: The ridge that extends along the margin of the compound eye and corresponds to the ocular suture.

detailed.name: clava
detailed.def: The anatomical cluster that is composed of apical flagellomeres bearing multiporous plates in female organism.

detailed.name: claval fold
detailed.def: The wing fold that is located on the fore wing, parallels the anal vein, and extends to the claval notch on the wing margin.

detailed.name: claval lobe
detailed.def: The wing region that is posterior to the claval furrow and anterior to the jugal fold.

detailed.name: cleaning brush
detailed.def: The setiferous patch that is used to clean anatomical structures.

detailed.name: ventral rim of clypeus
detailed.def: The rim that extends along the distal margin of the clypeus.

detailed.name: clypeal process
detailed.def: The process that is located medially on the clypeus.

detailed.name: clypeo-pleurostomal line
detailed.def: The line that extends from the anterior tentorial pit to the cranial margin and separates the clypeus laterally from the pleurostoma.

detailed.name: clypeus
detailed.def: The area that corresponds to the site of origin of the clypeo-epipharyngeal muscle.

detailed.name: colleterial gland
detailed.def: The glandula mucosa that secretes an adhesive substance used to fasten the eggs to a support.

detailed.name: protibial comb
detailed.def: The row of setae that extends along the apical margin of the fore tibia inbetween the articulations of the basitarsus and the calcar.

detailed.name: comb of the spur
detailed.def: The row that is located on the calcar and is composed of hairlike spines.

detailed.name: compound eye
detailed.def: The compound organ that is composed of ommatidia.

detailed.name: condylar carina of the mandible
detailed.def: The carina that is longitudinal and extends posteriorly on the lateral surface of the mandible from the mandibular condyle.

detailed.name: condylar groove
detailed.def: The impression that extends ventrally of the condylar carina of the mandible.

detailed.name: condyle
detailed.def: The articular surface that is convex and is inserted into the fossa of an adjacent sclerite.

detailed.name: conjunctiva
detailed.def: The area of the cuticle that is more flexible than adjacent sclerites.

detailed.name: corbicula
detailed.def: The area that is located on the metatibia and is delimited by a fringe of setae arising from the metatibial margins.

detailed.name: corner
detailed.def: The projection that is located at the intersection of two or more edges.

detailed.name: coronal suture
detailed.def: The suture that is located medially and is part of the epicranial suture.

detailed.name: costa
detailed.def: The wing vein that is anterior to the subcosta and is connected to the humeral plate.

detailed.name: costal cell
detailed.def: The membranous region of the forewing anterior to the submarginal vein, measured from the basal constriction that delimits the apex of the humeral plate of the wing to the point at which the submarginal vein touches the leading edge of the wing.

detailed.name: costal notch
detailed.def: The short conjunctiva that is located on a wing vein at the anterior wing margin.

detailed.name: coxa
detailed.def: The leg segment that is connected to the body and to the trochanter via conjunctivae and muscles.

detailed.name: coxal foramen
detailed.def: The foramen that is located lateroventrally of the pectus, in which the coxa is positioned.

detailed.name: coxal corium
detailed.def: The conjunctiva that connects the coxa to the pectus.

detailed.name: cranial condyle
detailed.def: The condyle that is located on the cranium.

detailed.name: cranio-antennal muscle
detailed.def: The muscle that arises from the cranium and inserts on the scape.

detailed.name: cranio-pharyngeal plate muscle
detailed.def: The head muscle that arises internally from the face and inserts on the pharyngeal plate.

detailed.name: cranium
detailed.def: The sclerite that is articulated with the cervical prominence, the scapes and the mandibles.

detailed.name: crossvein
detailed.comment: There are no crossveins in Hymenoptera.

detailed.name: cubital vein
detailed.def: The longitudinal vein that is posterior to the marginal vein.

detailed.name: cupula
detailed.def: The sclerite that is connected via conjunctiva and attached via muscles to abdominal tergum 9 and the gonostyle/volsella complex.

detailed.name: cuspis
detailed.def: The projection that is located apicolaterally on the parossiculus and is adjacent to the digitus.

detailed.name: cuticle
detailed.def: The acellular anatomical structure that is the external layer of the integument (covers the entire body surface as well as lines ectodermal invaginations such as the stomodeum, proctodeum and tracheae) and produced by the epidermal cells.

detailed.name: depression
detailed.def: The area that is external, concave, point-like and does not correspond to an apodeme.

detailed.name: dicondylic joint
detailed.def: The joint that is composed of two sclerites connected with to articulations.

detailed.name: dilator muscle apodeme
detailed.def: The apodeme that corresponds to the site of origin of the dilator muscle of the propodeal spiracle.

detailed.name: discrimen
detailed.def: The sulcus that corresponds with the discrimenal lamella.

detailed.name: discrimenal lamella
detailed.def: The ridge that is median, longitudinal and arises anteriorly from the base of the furca.

synonyms.name: vertical plate of the sternal apophysis

detailed.name: apical gonostyle/volsella complex-harpal muscle
detailed.def: The gonostyle/volsellal complex-harpal muscle that arises from the distolateral margin of the gonostyle/volsellal complex and inserts on the lateral wall of the harpe.

detailed.name: distal gonostipo-parossiculal muscle
detailed.def: The gonostyle/parossiculal muscle that arises distally of the lateral part of the gonostipes and inserts on the distal part of the parossiculus distally of the site of origin of the proximal gonostipo-parossiculal muscle.

detailed.name: distal tarsal plantula
detailed.def: The tarsal plantula that arises distally on the tarsomere.

detailed.name: distivolsellar apodeme
detailed.def: The apodeme that is located distally on the parossiculus and is the site of insertion of the gonostyle/volsella complex-gonostyle muscle.

detailed.name: distodorsal gonostyle/volsella complex-penisvalval muscle
detailed.def: The dorsal gonostyle/volsella complex-penisvalval muscle that arises distodorsally from the gonostyle volsella complex and inserts on the proximal region of the penisvalva.

detailed.name: distoventral gonostyle/volsella complex-penisvalval muscle
detailed.def: The ventral gonostyle/volsella complex-penisvalval muscle that arises from the proximoventral part of the gonostyle/volsella complex, inserts medially on the penisvalva and is oriented distodorsally.

detailed.name: dorsal axillar area
detailed.def: The area that is located medially on the axilla and is delimited laterally by the axillar carina and posteromedially by scutoscutellar sulcus.

detailed.name: dorsal incision of the pronotum
detailed.def: The notch that is located submedially on the dorsal margin of the pronotum and fits with the anterior extension of the preaxilla.

detailed.name: dorsal mesofurco-profurcal muscle
detailed.def: The profurcal muscle that originates from the lateral part of the lateral mesofurcal arm and inserts on the posterior surface of the posterior profurcal lamella.

detailed.name: dorsal mesometapleural carina
detailed.def: The carina that extends along the dorsal margin of the mesometapleuron.

detailed.name: dorsal mesopleural inflection
detailed.def: The inflection that extends along the dorsal margin of the mesopleuron anterior to the subalar pit and articulates with the second axillary sclerite.

detailed.name: dorsal mesopostnotal flange
detailed.def: The area that is more heavily sclerotised than surrounding areas, traverse the mesopostnotum, unites laterally with the dorsal mesopostnotal flange, continuous laterally with the laterophragma and dorsally (anteriorly) with the pseudophragma and is located anterior to the ventral mesopostnotal flange.

detailed.name: dorsal mesopostnotal incision
detailed.def: The notch that is situated medially on the dorsal margin of the mesopostnotum.

detailed.name: dorsal metafurcal lamella
detailed.def: The ridge that extends along the lateral metafurcal arm and corresponds to the site of origin of the metafurco-second abdominal sternal muscle.

detailed.name: dorsal metapleural area
detailed.def: The area that is delimited posterodorsally by the metapleural carina and anteroventrally by the metapleural sulcus.

detailed.name: dorsal pharyngeal dilatator
detailed.def: The pharyngeal muscle that arises on the inner proximal surface of the upper face, laterally to the origin of the anterior fronto-labral muscle, but mesally to the posterior fronto-labral muscle.

detailed.name: dorsal plate
detailed.comment: No definition or reference, unclear concept.

detailed.name: dorsal premental adductor
detailed.def: The tentorio-labial muscle that arises from the cranium and inserts distally on the labium adjacent to the level of the salivary orifice.

detailed.name: dorsal profurcal lamella
detailed.def: The ridge that extends dorsally on the profurcal arm and corresponds to the site of origin of the dorsal and ventral profurco-postoccipital muscles.

detailed.name: dorsal profurco-postoccipital muscle
detailed.def: The postoccipital muscle that arises from the dorsal part of the lateral area of the dorsal profurcal lamella and inserts on the postocciput.

detailed.name: dorsal pronotal area
detailed.def: The area that is delimited ventrally by the pronotal suprahumeral sulcus and corresponds to the site of origin of the pronoto-procoxal, pronoto-postoccipital, pronoto-laterocervical and the pronoto-propleural muscles.

detailed.name: dorsal pronotal corner
detailed.def: The corner that is located submedially on the dorsal margin of the pronotum and that is adjacent to the anterolateral mesoscutal corner.

detailed.name: dorsal pronotal inflection
detailed.def: The inflection that extends along the dorsal margin of the pronotum and supports the anterior inflection of the mesoscutum.

detailed.name: dorsal propleural area
detailed.def: The area that is located posterodorsally of the well-sclerotized lateral propleural area and usually differs from it in sculpture.

detailed.name: dorsal propleural ridge
detailed.def: The ridge that is marginal or submarginal, extends along the dorsal margin of the propleuron and corresponds to the site of origin of the lateral propleuro-postoccipital muscle.

detailed.name: dorsal propodeal inflection
detailed.def: The inflection that extends along the dorsal margin of the propodeum and accommodates the metanotum.

detailed.name: dorsal rod
detailed.def: The sclerite that is located dorsally between the penisvalvae.

detailed.name: dorsal premento-salivarial muscle
detailed.def: The salivarial muscle that inserts proximodorsally on the salivary duct.

detailed.name: dorsal tentorial arm
detailed.def: The apodeme that arises anterodorsally from the anterior tentorial arm and extends to the anterior wall of the cranium.

detailed.name: dorsal tooth
detailed.def: The apical tooth of mandible that is located closest to the clypeus.

detailed.name: dorso-pleural line
detailed.def: The anatomical line that is located between the dorsum and the pleuron.

detailed.name: dorsolateral cupulo-gonostyle/volsella complex muscle
detailed.def: The cupulo-gonostyle/volsella complex muscle that arises from the dorsolateral part of the cupula, just laterally of the site of origin of the dorsomedian cupulo-gonostipal muscle, and inserts on the dorsolateral part of the gonostipes.

detailed.name: dorsomedial cupulo-gonostyle/volsella complex muscle
detailed.def: The cupulo-gonostyle/volsella complex muscle that inserts medially on the dorsal region of the gonostyle/volsella complex.

detailed.name: dorsope
detailed.def: The pit that is located at the proximal end of the dorsal carina on the abdominal tergum 2.

detailed.name: dorsum
detailed.def: The area of the segment that is dorsal.

detailed.name: duct
detailed.def: The cavitated compound organ that is canal-like, layered with epithelial cells and leads from an exocrine gland or organ.

detailed.name: ductus ejaculatorius
detailed.def: The duct that connects the vas deferens with the endophallus and is ectodermal in origin.

detailed.name: ectophallus
detailed.def: The anatomical cluster that is composed of the sclerites and muscles of the external male genitalia.

detailed.name: edge
detailed.def: The margin that extends along the border of two areas that are oriented differently.

detailed.name: anellus
detailed.def: The flagellomere that is located proximally on the flagellum and lacks longitudinal sensilla.

detailed.name: encircling groove
detailed.def: The anatomical space that surrounds the plate-like structure in a multiporous plate sensillum.

detailed.name: endocuticle
detailed.def: The procuticle that is not tanned (sclerotised, proteins scarcely crosslinked) and located between the Schmidt's layer and the exocuticle.

detailed.name: endophallus
detailed.def: The conjunctiva that connects the gonopore with the penisvalvae.

detailed.name: epicnemial carina
detailed.def: The carina that delimits posteriorly the epicnemium.

detailed.name: epicnemial pit
detailed.def: The cuticular invagination that is located at the lateral end of the epicnemial carina.

detailed.name: epicnemium
detailed.def: The scrobe that is located anteroventrally on the mesopectus and accommodates the procoxa.

detailed.name: epicoxal lobe
detailed.def: The area that is located posteroventrally on the propleuron and is delimited anteriorly by the epicoxal sulcus.

detailed.name: epicoxal sulcus
detailed.def: The sulcus that is dorsal to the coxal foramen.

detailed.name: epicranial suture
detailed.def: The suture that is Y-shaped and located dorsally on the cranium.

detailed.name: epidermis
detailed.def: The epithelium that is located in the integument and composed of epidermal cells.

detailed.name: epimeron
detailed.def: The area that is located on the pleuron and is delimited anteriorly by the pleural sulcus.

detailed.name: epipharyngeal wall
detailed.def: The conjunctiva that extends between the distal margin of the labrum and the proximal boundary of the cibarium.

detailed.name: epipharynx
detailed.def: The lobe that is located distally on the epipharyngeal wall.

detailed.name: epipygium
detailed.def: The flange that is located on abdominal tergum 10, between the cerci.

detailed.name: episternum
detailed.def: The area that is located on the pleuron anteroventrally of the pleural sulcus.

detailed.name: epistomal declivity
detailed.def: The notch that is located on the ventral margin of the clypeus.

detailed.name: epistomal ridge
detailed.def: The ridge that corresponds to the epistomal sulcus.

detailed.name: epistomal sulcus
detailed.def: The sulcus that extends along the epistomal line.

detailed.name: epomial carina
detailed.def: The carina that is vertical, crosses the pronotum and separates the lateral pronotal area from the median pronotal area.

detailed.name: ergot
detailed.def: The apodeme that is lateral, located medially on the penisvalva and corresponds to the sites of insertion of the lateral and distoventral gonostyle/volsella complex-penisvalval muscle and the parossiculo-penisvalval muscles.

detailed.name: eusternum
detailed.def: The sclerite that is located on the thoracic sternum posteriorly of the spinasternum.

detailed.name: evaporatorium
detailed.def: The area that is highly sculptured and surrounds the opening of a gland.

detailed.name: exocuticle
detailed.def: The procuticle that is tanned (sclerotised, proteins are highly crosslinked) and located between the endocuticle and epicuticle.

detailed.name: external male genitalia
detailed.def: The appendage that is connected to the body by the cupulo-S9 muscles.

detailed.name: external paramera
detailed.def: The anatomical cluster that is composed of the gonostipites and volsellae.

detailed.name: premento-palpal muscle
detailed.def: The labial muscle that arises from the prementum and inserts on the first sclerite of the labial palp.

detailed.name: exuvia
detailed.def: The cuticle that is discarded after a molt.

detailed.name: face
detailed.def: The area that extends from the median ocellus to the oral foramen.

detailed.name: facial fovea
detailed.def: The area that is concave, narrow, often finely and densely pubescent and extends along the inner orbit.

detailed.name: facial orbit
detailed.def: The line that extends along the border of the eye and lower face.

detailed.name: facial pit
detailed.def: The pit that overlaps with the facial apodeme.

detailed.name: facial sulcus
detailed.def: The sulcus that arises medially from the ventral margin of the median ocellus and extends towards the intertorular carina.

detailed.name: fascia
detailed.def: The band that is transverse and composed of setae in Apoidea.

detailed.name: felt field
detailed.def: The setiferous patch that is located sublaterally on an abdominal sternum.

detailed.name: felt line
detailed.def: The band that is composed of flattened setae and secretory pores and is located laterally on abdominal tergum 3.

detailed.name: female genitalia
detailed.def: The genitalia that is part of the female organism.

detailed.name: femoral depression
detailed.comment: Concept miss-interpreted based on label only, interpretation doesn't exist in real life.

detailed.name: femoral groove
detailed.def: The scrobe that is located on the mesopleuron into which the mesofemur fits when pressed against the mesosoma.

detailed.name: femur
detailed.def: The leg segment that is distal to the trochanter and proximal to the tibia.

detailed.name: fibula ducti
detailed.def: The sclerite that is located in the proximal end of the unpaired part of the ductus ejaculatorius.

detailed.name: first abdominal spiracle
detailed.def: The abdominal spiracle that is the anteriormost in the body.

detailed.name: first axillary sclerite
detailed.def: The axillary sclerite that articulates with the mesonotum.

detailed.name: first median plate
detailed.def: The sclerite that is located between the second axillary and the distal arm of the third axillary at the base of the mediocubital field.

detailed.name: first mesopleuro-mesonotal muscle
detailed.def: The mesonotal muscle that arises ventrally from the mesopectus and inserts on the mesoscutum laterally of the notauli and medially of the parascutal carina.

detailed.name: first phragmo-postoccipital muscle
detailed.def: The postoccipital muscle that originates from the first phragma and inserts on the postocciput.

detailed.name: first phragmo-propleural muscle
detailed.def: The propleural muscle that arises medially from the prophragma and inserts on the posterodorsal margin of the propleuron.

detailed.name: first phragmo-second phragmal muscle
detailed.def: The mesophragmal muscle that extends between the prophragma and mesophragma.

detailed.name: distal gonostyle/volsella complex-harpal muscle
detailed.def: The gonostyle/volsella complex-harpal muscle that inserts on the median wall of the harpe and arises distally of the site of origin of the proximal gonostyle/volsella complex-harpal muscle.

detailed.name: first subdiscal
detailed.def: The wing cell that is located on the wing.

detailed.name: first valvifer
detailed.def: The area of the first valvifer-first valvula complex that is proximal to the aulax, bears the ninth tergal condyle of the first valvifer and the second valviferal condyle of the first valvifer and is connected to the genital membrane by muscle.

detailed.name: first valvula
detailed.def: The area of the first valvifer-first valvula complex that is delimited distally by the proximal margin of the aulax.

detailed.name: fissure
detailed.def: The line that secondarily divides a sclerite.

detailed.name: flabellum
detailed.def: The sclerite that is located at the apex of the glossa in Apoidea.

detailed.name: flagellomere
detailed.def: The annulus that is located distally of the pedicel.

detailed.name: flagellum
detailed.def: The anatomical cluster composed of flagellomeres.

detailed.name: flange
detailed.def: The projection that is lamella-like and is located on a rim, carina, apodeme or edge.

detailed.name: foramen
detailed.def: The anatomical space that is surrounded by sclerites and allows for the passage of haemolymph, nerves and tracheae.

detailed.name: foramen genitale
detailed.def: The anatomical space that is surrounded by the proximal margin of the cupula.

detailed.name: occipital foramen
detailed.def: The foramen that is delimited dorsally by the postocciput.

detailed.name: fore leg
detailed.def: The leg that is located on the prothorax.

detailed.name: fore tibia
detailed.def: The tibia that is located on the fore leg.

detailed.name: fore wing
detailed.def: The wing that is located on the mesothorax.

detailed.name: fossa
detailed.def: The articular surface that is concave and accommodates the condyle of another sclerite.

detailed.name: frenal line
detailed.def: The line that is transverse, arched anteriorly and is continuous with the posterior margins of the mesoscutellar arms.

detailed.name: frenum
detailed.def: The area that is located posteriorly on the mesoscutellum and is delimited anteriorly by the frenal line.

detailed.name: frontal line
detailed.def: The line that is median and extending on the frons.

detailed.name: frontal orbit
detailed.def: The area that extends along the lateral margin of the upper face.

detailed.name: frontal patch
detailed.def: The setiferous patch that is located on the frons.

detailed.name: frontal suture
detailed.def: The suture that diverges ventrally (or anteriorly) from the coronal suture between the antennal foramina toward the anterior articulations of the mandibles.

detailed.name: functional mouth
detailed.def: The anatomical space that is delimited posteriorly by the distal part of the sitophore and anteriorly by the tormae.

detailed.name: funicle
detailed.def: The anatomical cluster that is composed of flagellomeres located between the pedicel and clava or the distalmost anellus and clava.

detailed.name: funicle segment
detailed.def: The annulus that is located in the funicle.

detailed.name: furca
detailed.def: The apodeme that arises medially from the ventral part of the thorax and corresponds to the site of origin of interfurcal and coxal muscles.

detailed.name: furcal arm
detailed.def: The furca that is paired, arises medioventrally from the sternum and corresponds to the furcal pit.

detailed.name: furcal pit
detailed.def: The pit that corresponds to the furca.

detailed.name: furcasternum
detailed.def: The area that is located posteriorly on the prosternum and bears the profurca.

detailed.name: galea
detailed.def: The lobe that is located on the stipes at the distal part of the posterior stipital sclerite distolateral to the lacinia.

detailed.name: gaster
detailed.def: The anatomical cluster that is composed of all abdominal segments posterior to abdominal segment 2.

detailed.name: gastrocoelus
detailed.def: The area that is concave, transverse, and located anterolaterally on abdominal tergum 3.

detailed.name: gena
detailed.def: The area that is delimited by the intersection of the interorbital plane, the margin of the compound eye, the margin of the oral foramen, the occipital carina and the malar sulcus.

detailed.name: genal patch
detailed.def: The setiferous patch that is located on the gena.

detailed.name: genitalia
detailed.def: The anatomical system that is involved in copulation, fertilization and/or oviposition.

detailed.name: gland
detailed.def: The anatomical cluster that is composed of epithelial cell(s) that secrete or excrete materials not related to their ordinary metabolic needs.

detailed.name: glossa
detailed.def: The lobe of the labium that is limited posteroproximally by the prementum, anteroproximally by the fold traversing the salivary orifice and laterally by the paraglossae.

detailed.name: premento-glossal muscle
detailed.def: The labial muscle that arises on the ventral part of the prementum, laterally to the ventral premento-salivarial muscle and inserts on the anterior glossal plate.

detailed.name: glymma
detailed.def: The groove that is located between the spiracle and the anterior margin of abdominal tergum 2.

detailed.name: gonad
detailed.def: The compound organ that makes gametes.

detailed.name: gonocondyle
detailed.def: The apophysis that is located ventromedially on the proximal margin of the cupula and is the site of insertion of the lateral S9-cupulal muscle.

detailed.name: gonoforceps
detailed.def: The sclerite that is located distally of the cupula and dorsolaterally of the volsella, surrounds the aedeagus dorsolaterally and does not connected to any sclerite distally.

detailed.name: gonomacula
detailed.def: The conjunctiva that is located at the distal apex of the harpe.

detailed.name: gonopod
detailed.def: The anatomical cluster that is composed of the gonostipites, volsellae and harpes.

detailed.name: gonossiculo-phallotremal muscle
detailed.def: The male genitalia muscle that arises from the basiura and inserts on the endophallus.

detailed.name: gonossiculus
detailed.def: The sclerite that is located on the distoventral part of the gonostyle/volsella complex, and is articulated with the more proximal sclerites of the gononstyle/volsella complex.

detailed.name: gonostipes
detailed.def: The sclerite that is located dorsolaterally on the gonostyle/volsella complex, is connected to the distal margin of the cupula, to the proximal margin of the harpe, and to the lateral margin of the volsella.

detailed.name: gonostipital arm
detailed.def: The apodeme that is located proximally on the ventral part of the gonostipes.

detailed.name: gonostipo-volsellal muscle
detailed.def: The male genitalia muscle that arises proximally from the ventral part of the gonostipes and inserts on the volsella at the border between the gono- and parossiculi.

detailed.name: gonostyle
detailed.def: The anatomical cluster that is composed of sclerites located distally of the cupula dorsolaterally of the volsella, and that surround the aedeagus.

synonyms.name: outer or central arm of stipes

detailed.name: gradulus
detailed.def: The carina that is transverse and posteriorly directed laterally and is located on the abdominal tergite.

detailed.name: gular ridge
detailed.comment: There is no gula in Hymenoptera.

detailed.name: gular sulcus
detailed.comment: There is no gula in Hymenoptera.

detailed.name: hamulus
detailed.def: The seta that is located on the anterior margin of the hind wing and interlocks with the recurved posterior edge of the fore wing in Hymenoptera during flight, making them functionally one winged.

detailed.name: harpe
detailed.def: The sclerite that is located distally on the gonostyle/volsella complex and does not connect to the cupula, and does not connect to the volsella by conjunctiva or muscles.

detailed.name: harpo-gonomaculal muscle
detailed.def: The male genitalia muscle that arises form the harpe and inserts on the gonomacula.

detailed.name: head
detailed.def: The tagma that is located anterior to the thorax.

detailed.name: head capsule
detailed.def: The anatomical cluster that is composed of the cranium and compound eye cuticle.

detailed.name: hind leg
detailed.def: The leg that is located on the metathorax.

detailed.name: hind wing
detailed.def: The wing that is located on the metathorax.

detailed.name: median complex
detailed.def: The anatomical cluster that is composed of median plates.

detailed.name: humeral plate
detailed.def: The sclerite that articulates with the proximal end of the costal vein.

detailed.name: humeral sclerite of the metanotum
detailed.def: The sclerite that is connected to the lateral margin of the metanotum and bears the anterior metanotal wing process.

detailed.name: hymenopteran
detailed.def: The multi-cellular organism that is classified in Hymenoptera.

detailed.name: hyperoccipital carina
detailed.def: The carina that extends on the vertex between the outer orbits.

detailed.name: hypoclypeal depression
detailed.def: The anatomical space that is delimited anteriorly by the clypeus and posteriorly by the labrum.

detailed.name: hypopharyngeal rod
detailed.def: The ligament that connects the proximolateral margin of the prementum with the proximal part of the ligula.

detailed.name: hypopharynx
detailed.def: The area that is delimited proximally by the proximal margin of the sitophore, distally by the salivarial orifice.

detailed.name: hypopygium
detailed.def: The abdominal sternum that is the posteriormost visible sclerite located ventrally in the abdomen.

detailed.name: hypostoma
detailed.def: The area that extends on the posterior (ventral) margin of the oral foramen along the site of attachments of the conjunctiva connecting the cranium with the maxillae and is delimited laterally by the pleurostomal fossa.

detailed.name: hypostomal bridge
detailed.def: The subforaminal bridge that is delimited laterally by the dorsally extended hypostomal carina.

detailed.name: hypostomal carina
detailed.def: The carina that delimits the hypostoma from the cranium.

detailed.name: hypostomal sulcus
detailed.def: The sulcus that extends along the posteroventral margin of the cranium between the pleurostomal fossae and delimits the hypostoma.

detailed.name: hypostomal tooth
detailed.def: The projection that is located on the posterolateral margin of the oral foramen at the junction of the hypostoma and the pleurostomal fossa.

detailed.name: impression
detailed.def: The groove that does not correspond to a ridge.

detailed.name: inflection
detailed.def: The anatomical cluster that is composed of a ridge that extends along the margin of a sclerite and the marginal area of the sclerite delimited from the sclerite by the ridge articulating with the inflection of another sclerite.

detailed.name: inner orbit
detailed.def: The line that extends along the border between the eye and face.

detailed.name: integrated tarsal plantula
detailed.def: The tarsal plantula that arises medially on the tarsomere.

detailed.name: integument
detailed.def: The anatomical system that forms the covering layer of the animal, ectodermal in origin and composed of epidermal cells producing the cuticle.

detailed.name: interantennal prominence
detailed.def: The area that is elevated and extends between the antennal rims.

detailed.name: interaxillar sulcus
detailed.def: The median sulcus of the mesonotum between the anterior margin of the mesoscutellar-axillar complex and the anterior margin of the mesoscutellum.

detailed.name: intercoxal space
detailed.def: The area that is located between the acetabular carina and the ventral mesopleural carina.

detailed.name: internal male genitalia
detailed.def: The compound organ that functions as producer and transporter of sperm, spermatophore and semen, composed of the testes, vas deferens, accessory glands and ductus ejaculatorius. Ecto- and mesodermal in origin.

detailed.name: internal metanotal ridge
detailed.def: The ridge that corresponds to the metascutellar arm.

detailed.name: interocellar pit
detailed.def: The pit that is located on the upper face between lateral ocelli and corresponds internally with a conical apodeme and not the tentorium.

detailed.name: interocellar space
detailed.def: The area that is limited posteriorly by the posterior ocellar line and laterally by the lateral ocellar line.

detailed.name: interommatidial seta
detailed.def: The seta that is located at the intersection of the margins of three ommatidiae.

detailed.name: interorbital space
detailed.def: The interocular distance that is the shortest between the inner margin of compound eyes.

detailed.name: interpenisvalval muscle
detailed.def: The male genitalia muscle that connects the valvurae.

detailed.name: intersegmental membrane
detailed.def: The conjunctiva that connects two segments.

detailed.name: intersegmental membrane-mesobasalar muscle
detailed.def: The mesobasalar muscle that arises from the intersegmental membrane between the pronotum and the mesopectus and inserts on the mesobasalare.

detailed.name: intersegmentalia
detailed.def: The sclerite that is located on the intersegmental membrane.

detailed.name: intersternite
detailed.def: The intersegmentalia that is located in between sterna.

detailed.name: interstice
detailed.def: The area that is unsculptured and is delimited by striae or punctures.

detailed.name: intertorular carina
detailed.def: The carina that extends between the dorsal margin of the antennal rims.

detailed.name: intervalvula
detailed.def: The sternal sclerite in the venter of the ninth abdominal segment between the second valvifers.

detailed.name: intervolsellal muscle
detailed.def: The male genitalia muscle that connects the proximal part of parossiculi.

detailed.name: intrinsic muscle of the 9th sternum
detailed.def: The sternal muscle that connects the lateral margins of the abdominal tergum 9 and extends internally of the external male genitalia.

detailed.name: intrinsic muscle of the gonostipes
detailed.def: The male genitalia muscle that connects the dorsal and ventral part of the gonostipes.

detailed.name: intrinsic palpal muscle
detailed.def: The labial muscle that arises from a palpal sclerite and inserts on the palpal sclerite distal to it.

detailed.name: jugal region
detailed.def: The wing region that is delimited by the plica jugalis.

detailed.name: juxtantennal carina
detailed.def: The carina that is longitudinal and mesal to the antennal base.

detailed.name: kleisiades
detailed.def: The area that is located apically on the gonostyle when it consist only from one sclerite and set off partly by conjunctiva. The kleisiades is never connected to the proximal part of the gonostyle via muscles.

detailed.name: labial endite
detailed.comment: Adds an unnecessary layer in the circumscription of glossa and paraglossa

detailed.name: labial gland
detailed.def: The gland that empties between the base of the hypopharynx and the labium, or on the hypopharynx.

detailed.name: labial palp
detailed.def: The anatomical structure that is distal to the proximal margin of the first sclerite of the labial palp.

detailed.name: labial suture
detailed.comment: The concept is misunderstood in this definition. For labial suture see HAO_0002147.

detailed.name: labiomaxillary complex
detailed.def: The anatomical cluster that is composed of the labium and maxillae.

detailed.name: labium
detailed.def: The appendage that is encircled by the area that is proximally delimited by the lateral margins of the cardo and the posterior stipital sclerite laterally, and the anatomical line that is tangential to the salivary duct and traverses the salivary orifice anteriorly.

detailed.name: labral compressor
detailed.def: The epipharyngeal muscle that arises from the proximal part of the labrum and inserts on the distal epipharyngeal wall.

detailed.name: labral seta
detailed.def: The seta that is located on the labrum.

detailed.name: labrum
detailed.def: The sclerite that is situated along the distal margin of the clypeus and is connected along its proximal margin with the distal margin of the epipharyngeal wall.

detailed.name: lacinial lobe
detailed.def: The lobe that extends proximally on the distal margin of the medial stipital wall, is adjacent to the basal lacinial sclerite, overlaps the proximal part of the galea.

detailed.name: larval abdomen
detailed.def: The larval tagma that is located posterior to the thorax.

detailed.name: larval head
detailed.def: The larval tagma that is located anterior to the thorax.

detailed.name: larval tagma
detailed.def: The tagma that is located in the larva.

detailed.name: larval thorax
detailed.def: The larval tagma that is composed of the prothorax, mesothorax and metathorax and is located between the head and the abdomen.

detailed.name: lateral S9-cupulal muscle
detailed.def: The S9-cupulal muscle that arises sublaterally from S9 and inserts medioventrally on the cupula.

detailed.name: lateral apodeme
detailed.def: The apodeme that is located laterally on the proximal margin of abdominal tergum 9.

detailed.name: lateral area of the mesoscutum
detailed.def: The area that is located between the notaulus and the parascutal carina.

detailed.name: lateral articular process
detailed.def: The projection that is located on the prolpeuron posterolaterally of the epicoxal lobe and bears the procoxal condyle of the propleuron.

synonyms.name: lateral coxal articular process of the propleuron

detailed.name: lateral axillar area
detailed.def: The area that is located laterally on the axilla and is delimited medially by the axillar carina.

detailed.name: lateral bar of the mesoscutellum
detailed.def: The bar that extends from the dorsal axillar area to the mesoscutellum.

detailed.name: lateral basisternal projection
detailed.def: The area that is located anterolaterally on the basisternum and forms the median propleural site of articulation with the procoxa.

detailed.name: lateral carina of abdominal tergite 3
detailed.def: The carina that extends along the lateral margin of abdominal tergite 3.

detailed.name: lateral gonostyle/volsella complex-penisvalval muscle
detailed.def: The gonostyle/volsella complex-penisvalval muscle that arises anterolaterally of the site of origin of the distodorsal gonostyle/volsella complex-penisvalval muscle and inserts laterally on the penisvalva.

detailed.name: median gonostyle/volsella complex-volsella muscle
detailed.def: The gonostyle/volsella complex-volsellal muscle that arises medially of the submedian conjunctiva on the distoventral margin of gonostyle/volsella complex.

synonyms.name: lateral intrinsic muscle of the volsella

detailed.name: lateral margin of mesoscutum
detailed.def: The inflection that extends along the anterolateral margin of the mesoscutum and fit with the dorsal pronotal inflection.

detailed.name: lateral mesofurcal arm
detailed.def: The apodeme that arises from the base of the mesofurca.

detailed.name: lateral mesofurco-mesotrochanteral muscle
detailed.def: The mesotrochanteral muscle that arises laterally from the lateral mesofurcal arm, laterally to the site of origin  of the median mesofurco-mesotrochanteral muscle and inserts on the mesotrochanteral apodeme.

detailed.name: lateral mesoscutellar spine
detailed.def: The projection that is located on the posterodorsal margin of the axillula.

detailed.name: lateral metapleuro-metacoxal muscle
detailed.def: The metacoxal muscle that originates ventrally of the metapleural ridge and inserts anterolaterally on the dorsal margin of the metacoxa.

detailed.name: lateral neck plate
detailed.comment: There is no cervical sclerite and hence no cervix in Hymenoptera.

detailed.name: lateral ocellar line
detailed.def: The anatomical line that is the shortest between the margins of the  median and lateral ocelli.

detailed.name: lateral ocellus
detailed.def: The ocellus that is paired.

detailed.name: lateral pronotal area
detailed.def: The area of the pronotum that is lateral and delimited medially by the epomial carina.

detailed.name: lateral propleural area
detailed.def: The area that is located laterally on the propleuron and is delimited by the longitudinal carina of the propleuron.

detailed.name: lateral propodeal area
detailed.def: The area that is located laterally on the propodeum, is delimited posteromedially by the lateral propodeal carina and is delimited laterally by the plica.

detailed.name: lateral propodeal carina
detailed.def: The carina that is oblique and arises submedially from the anterior margin of the metapectal-propodeal complex and extends to the posterior propodeal projection.

detailed.name: lateral scutellar keel
detailed.def: The carina which is present on the lateral margin of the mesoscutellum.

detailed.name: lateral scutellar pit
detailed.def: The pit that is located along the posterolateral margin of the mesoscutellum.

detailed.name: lateral suspensor of the mouth
detailed.def: The hypopharyngeal muscle that arises from the distal end of the anterior tentorial arm or from the inner surface of the head capsule and inserts on the lateral surface of the proximolateral processes of the sitophore.

detailed.name: laterocervico-procoxal muscle
detailed.def: The procoxal muscle that arises from the cervical apodeme and inserts on the procoxa on the opposite side from which it arises.

detailed.name: laterope
detailed.def: The depression that is located laterally and proximally on abdominal tergum 2.

detailed.name: laterophragma
detailed.def: The apodeme that is located laterally on the postnotum.

detailed.name: laterotergite
detailed.def: The tergite that is located laterally on the abdominal tergum.

detailed.name: leg
detailed.def: The appendage that is composed of the coxa and all distal leg segments and is connected to the pectus.

detailed.name: leg segment
detailed.def: The segment that is part of a leg.

detailed.name: ligula
detailed.def: The anatomical cluster that is composed of the glossa and paraglossae.

detailed.name: longitudinal carina of the propleuron
detailed.def: The carina that extends along the lateral margin of the ventral propleural area.

key: longitudinal line of the dorsal profurcal lamella

detailed.name: longitudinal line of the dorsal profurcal lamella
detailed.def: The line that crosses the dorsal profurcal lamella and corresponds to the site of origin of the anterior profurcal lamella.

detailed.name: longitudinal metacoxal carina
detailed.def: The carina that is longitudinal and is located on the metacoxa.

key: longitudinal muscle of the sucking pump

detailed.name: longitudinal muscle of the sucking pump
detailed.def: The epipharyngeal muscle that is unpaired and arises from the distal part of the pharyngeal wall and inserts along most of the length of the proximal epipharyngeal wall.

detailed.name: longitudinal vein
detailed.def: The anatomical cluster that is composed of abscissae extending along the long axis of the wing.

detailed.name: lower face
detailed.def: The area that is limited dorsally by the ventral margin of the antennal foramen laterally by the malar sulcus and ventrally by the oral foramen.

detailed.name: malar line
detailed.def: The anatomical line that is the shortest between the lower orbit and the ventrolateral margin of the cranium.

detailed.name: malar sulcus
detailed.def: The sulcus that extends between the ventral margin of the compound eye and the base of the mandible.

detailed.name: male genitalia
detailed.def: The genitalia that is part of the male organism.

detailed.name: mandible
detailed.def: The appendage that is encircled by one sclerite that is connected to the cranium proximolaterally and to the maxillo-labial complex proximomedially via conjunctivae and articulates with the cranium via the anterior and posterior cranio-mandibular articulations.

detailed.name: mandibular adductor muscle apodeme
detailed.def: The apodeme that corresponds to the site of insertion of the mandibular adductor muscle.

detailed.name: mandibular condyle
detailed.def: The condyle that is located ventrally (posteriorly) on the proximolateral edge of the mandible and inserts into the pleurostomal fossa.

detailed.name: mandibular gland
detailed.def: The gland that opens mesally at the base of the mandible.

detailed.name: margin
detailed.def: The line that delimits the periphery of an area.

detailed.name: marginal cilia
detailed.def: The row of setae that is located along the margin of the wing blade in the same plane as the wing blade.

detailed.name: marginal vein
detailed.def: The abscissa that is located along the anterior margin of the fore wing and is thought to correspond to the anterior abscissa of the radius (R1).

detailed.name: maxilla
detailed.def: The appendage that is encircled by the area that is proximally delimited by the hypostoma posteriorly, the median margin of the mandible laterally, the labrum anterolaterally and the labium medially.

detailed.name: maxillary gland
detailed.def: The gland that opens medially at the bases of the maxillae.

detailed.name: maxillary palp
detailed.def: The anatomical structure that is distal to the proximal margin of the first sclerite of the maxillary palp.

detailed.name: medial S9-cupulal muscle
detailed.def: The male genitalia muscle that arises from the spiculum and inserts on the gonocondyle.

detailed.name: gonostyle/volsella complex-gonossiculus muscle
detailed.def: The gonossiculal muscle that arises ventromedially from the gonostyle/volsella complex and inserts laterally on the gonossiculus.

synonyms.name: medial intrinsic muscle of the volsella

detailed.name: medial propodeal furrow
detailed.def: The impression that is longitudinal and located medially on the abdominal tergum 1.

detailed.name: medial vein
detailed.def: The wing vein that is posterior to the radial vein and is connected to the anterior margin of the anterior median plate.

detailed.name: median area of the mesoscutum
detailed.def: The area that is located between the notauli.

detailed.name: median mesofurco-mesotrochanteral muscle
detailed.def: The mesotrochanteral muscle that arises from the ventral surface of the lateral mesofurcal arm medial to the site of origin of the lateral mesofurco-mesotrochanteral muscle and inserts on the mesotrochanteral apodeme.

detailed.name: median mesoscutal line
detailed.def: The line that is located in the anteroposterior median line of the mesoscutum.

detailed.name: median mesoscutal sulcus
detailed.def: The sulcus that is a median mesoscutal line and that is adjacent to the (internal) median mesoscutal ridge.

detailed.name: median metapleuro-metacoxal muscle
detailed.def: The metacoxal muscle that arises from the paracoxal ridge and from the metadiscrimenal lamella and inserts anterolaterally on the dorsal margin of the metacoxa.

detailed.name: median oblique line
detailed.def: The depression that is oblique and located submedially on the mesopleuron between the fore coxa and posterolateral corner of the mesopleuron.

detailed.name: median ocellus
detailed.def: The ocellus that is located medially on the cranium.

detailed.name: median profurco-procoxal muscle
detailed.def: The procoxal muscle that arises partly from the posteroventral surface of the ventral profurcal lamella and partly from the ventral surface of the profurcal arm.

detailed.name: median propodeal carina
detailed.def: The carina that is oriented anteroposteriorly and is located medially on the propodeum.

detailed.name: median sclerotized style
detailed.def: The sclerite that is located ventrally between the penisvalvae.

detailed.name: median sulcus of postgenal bridge
detailed.def: The sulcus that is located on the postgenal suture.

detailed.name: mediolateral S9-cupulal muscle
detailed.def: The cupulal muscle that arises medially from abdominal sternum 9 and inserts ventrolaterally on the cupula.

detailed.name: mentum
detailed.comment: The mentum and submentum are subdivisions of he postmentum in Coleoptera and Orthopteroidea. These sclerites are absent from Hymenoptera.

detailed.name: meron
detailed.def: The area that is located posterolaterally and proximally on the coxa, proximal to the basicostal suture.

detailed.name: mesepimeral inflection
detailed.def: The inflection that extends along the dorsal margin of the mesopectus.

detailed.name: mesepimeral ridge
detailed.def: The ridge that extends along the posterior margin of the mesopectus.

detailed.name: mesepimeral sulcus
detailed.def: The sulcus that extends along the posterior margin of the mesopectus, delimits the mesepimeral area and corresponds to the mesepimeral ridge.

detailed.name: mesepimeron
detailed.def: The epimeron that is located on the mesopleuron.

detailed.name: mesepisternal groove
detailed.def: A groove on the mesopleuron, extending ventrally from a pit under the base of the forewings and, when complete, reaching the anteroventral margin of the metathorax.

detailed.name: mesepisternum
detailed.def: The episternum that is located on the mesopleuron.

detailed.name: mesobasalare
detailed.def: The basalare that is located in the mesothorax.

detailed.name: mesocoxal depression
detailed.def: The acetabulum that is located posteroventrally on the mesopectus, accommodates the mesocoxa and is delimited anterolaterally by the postpectal carina.

detailed.name: mesocoxo-mesosubalar muscle
detailed.def: The mesosubalare muscle that arises from the mesocoxa and inserts on the mesosubalare.

detailed.name: mesodiscrimen
detailed.def: The discrimen that is located in the mesothorax and corresponds with the mesodiscrimenal lamella.

detailed.name: mesodiscrimenal lamella
detailed.def: The discrimenal lamella that is located in the mesothorax.

detailed.name: mesofurca
detailed.def: The furca that is not paired, arises from the mesopectus and continuous with the mesodiscrimenal lamella.

detailed.name: mesofurcal bridge
detailed.def: The apodeme that connects the lateral mesofurcal arms and corresponds to the site of origin of the dorsal mesofurco-profurcal muscle.

detailed.name: mesofurcal pit
detailed.def: The furcal pit that is located in the mesothorax.

detailed.name: mesofurco-mesocoxal muscle
detailed.def: The mesocoxal muscle that originates from the mesofurca and inserts on the mesocoxa.

detailed.name: mesofurco-mesolaterophragmal muscle
detailed.def: The mesolaterophragmal muscle that arises from the mesofurca and inserts on the mesolaterophragma.

detailed.name: mesofurco-metabasalar muscle
detailed.def: The metabasalar muscle that originates from the mesofurca and inserts on the metabasalare.

detailed.name: mesometapleural sulcus
detailed.def: The sulcus that crosses the mesometapleuron from the posterolateral margin of the mesocoxal foramen to the mesopleural pit.

detailed.name: mesometapleural suture
detailed.def: The line of separation that is located between the mesopectus and the metapectus.

detailed.name: mesometapleuron
detailed.def: The sclerite that is comprised of the fused meso- and metapleura.

detailed.name: mesonotum
detailed.def: The area that is limited anteriorly by the pronotum, laterally by the basalare, axillary sclerites, subalare and the mesopectus and posterolaterally by the mesopostnotum and the metanotum.

detailed.name: mesopectus
detailed.def: The sclerite that is U-shaped in cross section, connected anteriorly with the pronotum and the propectus, dorsally with the basalare, the mesonotum, the second axillary sclerite and the subalare, posteriorly with the metapectus and bears the mesodiscrimenal lamella and the mesofurca.

detailed.name: mesophragma
detailed.def: The phragma that extends along the ventral (posterior) margin of the ventral mesopostnotal flange and corresponds to the site of origin of the prophragmo-mesophragmal and the mesophragmo-metaphragmal muscles.
