detailed.name: mesopleural carina
detailed.def: The carina that crosses the mesopleuron and limits ventrally the femoral depression.

detailed.name: mesopleural epicoxal sulcus
detailed.def: The epicoxal sulcus that is located on the mesopleuron.

detailed.name: mesopleural triangle
detailed.def: The area that is limited dorsally by the subalar ridge, posteroventrally by the speculum and ends posteriorly in the subalar pit.

detailed.name: mesopleuro-mesobasalar muscle
detailed.def: The mesobasalar muscle that originates anteroventrally from the mesopleuron and insert on the mesobasalare.

detailed.name: mesopleuro-mesocoxal muscle
detailed.def: The mesocoxal muscle that arises from the mesopleuron and inserts on the mesocoxa.

key: mesopleuro-third axillary sclerite of hind wing muscle

detailed.name: mesopleuro-third axillary sclerite of hind wing muscle
detailed.def: The hind wing third axillary muscle that arises from the mesopleuron and inserts on the third axillary sclerite of hind wing.

synonyms.name: mesopleuro-third axillary sclerite of hind wing

relationships.target_name: third axillary sclerite of the hind wing

detailed.name: mesopleuron
detailed.def: The area that is located laterally of the mesodiscrimen.

detailed.name: mesopostnotum
detailed.def: The sclerite that bears the mesophragma and the mesolaterophragma.

detailed.name: mesopostscutellum
detailed.def: The area that is located posteromedially on the  mesoscutellum, is delimited anteriorly by the posterior scutellar depression.

detailed.name: mesoscutal humeral sulcus
detailed.def: The sulcus that extends medially along the parascutal carina and corresponds to a shallow ridge.

detailed.name: mesoscutal suprahumeral sulcus
detailed.def: The sulcus that extends along the anterior margin of the mesoscutum between the anterior-most point of the preaxilla and the anteroadmedian line and corresponds to the vertical lobe of the mesoscutum.

detailed.name: mesoscutellar-axillar complex
detailed.def: The area that is located posteriorly of the transscutal line and is composed of the axillae and the mesoscutellum.

detailed.name: mesoscutello-metanotal muscle
detailed.def: The metanotal muscle that arises from the mesonotum and inserts on the metanotum.

detailed.name: mesoscutellum
detailed.def: The scutellum that is located on the mesonotum.

detailed.name: mesoscutum
detailed.def: The scutum that is located on the mesonotum.

detailed.name: mesosoma
detailed.def: The anatomical cluster that is composed of the prothorax, mesothorax and the metapectal-propodeal complex.

detailed.name: mesospinasterno-metafurcal muscle
detailed.def: The metafurcal muscle that arises from the mesospinasternum and inserts on the metafurca.

detailed.name: mesosterno-mesocoxal muscle
detailed.def: The mesocoxal muscle that originates at least partly from the mesodiscrimenal lamella and inserts on the mesocoxa.

detailed.name: mesosterno-procoxal muscle
detailed.def: The procoxal muscle that arises anteriorly from the mesodiscrimenal lamella and inserts posteriorly on the procoxa.

detailed.name: mesosternum
detailed.def: The eusternum that is located in the mesothorax.

detailed.name: mesosubalare
detailed.def: The subalare that is located in the mesothorax.

detailed.name: anterior thoracic spiracle
detailed.def: The spiracle that is located on the border of the pronotum and mesopleuron.

detailed.name: mesothorax
detailed.def: The thoracic segment that is located between the prothorax and the metathorax and is delimited by the mesopectus and the mesonotum.

detailed.name: mesotrochantin
detailed.def: The trochantin that is located in the mesothorax.

detailed.name: metabasalare
detailed.def: The basalare that is located in the metathorax.

detailed.name: metacoxa
detailed.def: The coxa that is located on the hind leg.

detailed.name: metacoxal depression
detailed.def: The acetabulum that is concave, is located ventrally on metapectal-propodeal complex and is delimited anteriorly by the transverse metepisternal carina and accommodates the base of the metacoxa.

detailed.name: metacoxo-metasubalar muscle
detailed.def: The metasubalare muscle that arises from the metacoxa and inserts on the metasubalare.

detailed.name: metadiscrimen
detailed.def: The discrimen that is located in the metathorax and corresponds to the metadiscrimenal lamella.

detailed.name: metadiscrimenal lamella
detailed.def: The discrimenal lamella that is located in the metathorax.

detailed.name: metafurca
detailed.def: The furca that is located medioventrally on the metapectus.

detailed.name: metafurcal arm
detailed.def: The apodeme that arises ventromedially form the metapectus, extends towards the metapleural apodeme and corresponds to the site of origin of metacoxal and metatrochanteral muscles.

detailed.name: metafurcal pit
detailed.def: The furcal pit that is located in the metathorax.

detailed.name: metafurco-metacoxal muscle
detailed.def: The metacoxal muscle that originates from the metafurca and inserts on the posterior margin of the metacoxa.

detailed.name: metafurco-metatrochanteral muscle
detailed.def: The metatrochanteral muscle that arises from the metafurca and inserts on the metatrochanteral apodeme.

detailed.name: metafurco-second abdominal sternal muscle
detailed.def: The second abdominal sternal muscle that arises from the metafurca and inserts on abdominal sternum 2.

detailed.name: metanotal spine
detailed.def: The median process on the metascutellum.

detailed.name: metanotal trough
detailed.def: The area that is concave, and is delimited medially by the metascutellum, laterally by the supraalar area and posteriorly by the metascutellar arm.

detailed.name: metanotal-propodeal sulcus
detailed.def: The sulcus that extends along the anterior margin of the fused metanotum and abdominal tergum 1 and delimit an anterior narrow, usually differently sculptured area.

detailed.name: metanoto-metatrochanteral muscle
detailed.def: The metatrochanteral muscle that originates from the humeral sclerite and inserts on the metatrochanteral apodeme.

detailed.name: metanotum
detailed.def: The alinotum that is located in the metathorax, is connected with the mesoscutellum and the mesopostnotum anteriorly and the acrotergite of the first abdominal tergum posteromedially.

detailed.name: metapectal-propodeal complex
detailed.def: The sclerite that is connected anteriorly with the mesopectus dorsally with the metanotum, the metabasalare, second axillary sclerite of the hind wing and the metasubalare and articulates with the metacoxae and the metasoma.

detailed.name: metapectus
detailed.def: The area that is located anteriorly of the metapleural sulcus and ridge.

detailed.name: metaphragma
detailed.def: The phragma that corresponds to the site of origin of the mesophragmo-metaphragmal and metaphragmo-second abdominal tergal muscles.

detailed.name: metapleural apodeme
detailed.def: The pleural apodeme that is connected with the metafurca.

detailed.name: metapleural arm
detailed.def: The area that is located anterodorsally on the metapleuron.

detailed.name: metapleural carina
detailed.def: The carina that delimits the metapleuron dorsally from the propodeum, extends from just ventral of the metapleural arm to the metacoxal articulation and passes anteroventral to the propodeal spiracle.

detailed.name: metapleural epicoxal carina
detailed.def: The carina that delimits posteriorly the metapleural triangle.

detailed.name: metapleural epicoxal sulcus
detailed.def: The epicoxal sulcus that extends anteriorly along and close to the metapleural epicoxal carina.

detailed.name: metapleural pit
detailed.def: The pleural pit that is corresponds to the metapleural apodeme.

detailed.name: metapleural ridge
detailed.def: The pleural ridge that corresponds to the metapleural sulcus.

detailed.name: metapleural sulcus
detailed.def: The line that corresponds with the metapleural ridge.

detailed.name: metapleural triangle
detailed.def: The area that is located anteroventrally on the metapectus, is delimited ventrally by the ventral carina of the metapleuron and is triangular.

detailed.name: metapleuro-metabasalar muscle
detailed.def: The metabasalar muscle that arises from the metapleuron and inserts on the metabasalare.

detailed.name: metapleuro-metanotal muscle
detailed.def: The metanotal muscle that arises from the metapleuron and inserts on the metanotum.

detailed.name: metapleuro-metasubalar muscle
detailed.def: The metasubalare muscle that arises from the metapleuron and inserts on the metasubalare.

detailed.name: metapleuro-metatrochanteral muscle
detailed.def: The metatrochanteral muscle that arises from the metapleuron and inserts on the metatrochanteral apodeme.

key: metapleuro-third axillary sclerite of hind wing muscle

detailed.name: metapleuro-third axillary sclerite of hind wing muscle
detailed.def: The hind wing third axillary muscle that originates from the metapleuron and inserts on the third axillary sclerite of the hind wing.

detailed.name: metapleuron
detailed.def: The area of the metapectal-propodeal complex that is located laterally of the metadiscrimen.

detailed.name: metapostnotum
detailed.comment: Based on the definition of the postnotum sensu Snodgrass there is no metapostnotum in Hymenoptera.

detailed.name: metascutellar arm
detailed.def: The area that is elevated and extends along the posterior margin of the metanotum from the metascutellum to the supraalar area.

detailed.name: metascutellar carina
detailed.def: The carina that delimits laterally the metascutellum.

detailed.name: metascutellum
detailed.def: The area that is located posteromedially on the metanotum, is delimited laterally by the metanotal trough and corresponds to the reservoir of the dorsal vessel.

detailed.name: metasoma
detailed.def: The tagma that is connected anteriorly to the metapectal-propodeal complex at the propodeal foramen and consists of abdominal segments.

detailed.name: metasomal depression
detailed.def: The acetabulum that is concave, surrounds the nucha and accommodates the base of the metasoma.

detailed.name: metasternum
detailed.def: The eusternum that is located in the metathorax.

detailed.name: metasubalare
detailed.def: The subalare that is located in the metathorax.

detailed.name: metathorax
detailed.def: The thoracic segment that is located between the mesothorax and the first abdominal tergum and is delimited by the metanotum and the metapectus.

detailed.name: metatibia
detailed.def: The tibia that is located on the hind leg.

detailed.name: metepimeron
detailed.def: The epimeron that is located on the metapleuron and is located posteriorly of the metapleural sulcus.

detailed.name: metepisternal depression
detailed.def: The scrobe that is located anteroventrally on the mesopectus and accommodates the mesocoxa.

detailed.name: metepisternum
detailed.def: The episternum that is located anteroventrally of the metapleural sulcus.

detailed.name: mid coxa
detailed.def: The coxa that is located on the mid leg.

detailed.name: mid leg
detailed.def: The leg that is located on the mesothorax.

detailed.name: midpit
detailed.def: The depression that is located posteromedially on the mesoscutum.

detailed.name: monocondylic joint
detailed.def: The joint that is composed of two sclerites that are connected with one articulation.

detailed.name: mouthparts
detailed.def: The anatomical cluster that is composed of the labrum, epipharyngeal wall, hypopharyngeal wall (including the sitophore), mandibles, maxillae, labium and conjunctivae connecting them.

detailed.name: multiporous plate sensillum
detailed.def: The sensillum that corresponds to a multiporous plate.

detailed.name: muscle
detailed.def: The portion of tissue that is composed of contractile fibers.

key: muscle bearing process of the metanotum

detailed.name: muscle bearing process of the metanotum
detailed.def: The apodeme that corresponds to the site of insertions of the lateral metapleuro-metanotal muscles.

detailed.name: musculus retractor unguis
detailed.def: The intrinsic leg muscle that originates within the tibia and femur and has a long tendon that inserts through the terminal tarsomere onto the unguitractor.

detailed.name: netrion
detailed.def: The area that is located posteroventrally on the pronotum and corresponds to the site of origin of first flexor of the fore wing muscle.

detailed.name: netrion apodeme
detailed.def: The ridge that extends along the anterior margin of the netrion.

detailed.name: netrion sulcus
detailed.def: The sulcus that  anteriorly delimits the netrion.

detailed.name: notaulus
detailed.def: The line that extends submedially along the mesoscutum and corresponds to the median border of the site of origin of the first mesopleuro-mesonotal muscle.

detailed.name: notch
detailed.def: The part of the margin of a sclerite that is concave.

detailed.name: notopleural suture
detailed.def: The suture that is located between the mesonotum and mesopleuron.

detailed.name: notum
detailed.def: The area that is located dorsally on the thoracic segment and limited laterally by the pleuron.

detailed.name: nucha
detailed.def: The area that is raised and surrounds the propodeal foramen.

detailed.name: occipital arch
detailed.def: The area that is located on the cranium and is delimited anteriorly by the occipital suture and posteriorly by the postoccipital sutures.

detailed.name: occipital carina
detailed.def: The carina that surrounds dorsolaterally the occiput.

detailed.name: propleural fossa of the cranium
detailed.def: The fossa that articulates with the cranial condyle of the propleuron.

detailed.name: occipital depression
detailed.def: The depression that is located submedially on the occiput, ventral to the occipital carina.

detailed.name: occipital sulcus
detailed.def: The sulcus that is paired, extends dorsally from the posterior tentorial pit towards lateral ocellus and corresponds with the occipital ridge.

detailed.name: occiput
detailed.def: The area that is concave and surrounds the postocciput.

detailed.name: occlusor muscle apodeme
detailed.def: The apodeme that corresponds to the site of origin of the occlusor muscle of the thoracic spiracle.

synonyms.name: occlusor muscle apodeme of anterior thoracic spiracle

detailed.name: ocellar fovea
detailed.def: The depression that extends along the lateral margin of a lateral ocellus or along anterior margin of the anterior ocellus.

detailed.name: ocellus
detailed.def: The multi-tissue structure that is located on the top of the head, composed of the corneal lens, pigment cell, rhabdoms and synaptic plexus.

detailed.name: ocular ocellar line
detailed.def: The anatomical line that is shortest and connects the compound eye and the lateral ocellus.

detailed.name: ocular suture
detailed.def: The sulcus that extends along the margin of compound eye and corresponds to the circumocular ridge.

detailed.name: ommatidium
detailed.def: One of the facets of the compound eye.

detailed.name: operculum
detailed.def: The sclerite that functions like a lid and covers the spiracular opening.

detailed.name: oral foramen
detailed.def: The foramen that is located anteriorly/ventrally on the cranium in which the mouthparts are positioned.

detailed.name: orbicula
detailed.def: The sclerite that is located proximodorsally on the pretarsus and connects the distodorsal margin of the telotarsus with the dorsal part of the arolium.

detailed.name: orbit
detailed.def: The margin of the compound eye.

detailed.name: orbital band
detailed.def: The band that extends along the medial margin of the eye.

detailed.name: outer carina of the mandible
detailed.def: The carina that is longitudinal and extends anteriorly on the lateral surface of the mandible.

detailed.name: outer groove
detailed.def: The impression that extends dorsally of the condylar carina of the mandible.

detailed.name: outer orbit
detailed.def: The line that extends along the border between the eye and gena.

detailed.name: ovipore
detailed.def: The anatomical space that receives sperm ejected from the aedeagus.

detailed.name: ovipositor
detailed.def: The anatomical cluster that is composed of the first valvulae, second valvulae, third valvulae, first valvifers, second valvifers and female T9.

detailed.name: ovipositor sheath
detailed.def: The anatomical cluster that is composed of the third valvulae.

detailed.name: paddle shaped seta
detailed.def: The sensillum with a paddle-shaped cuticular componenet on the probasitarsus.

detailed.name: palpifer
detailed.def: The area that is located on the stipes and which articulates with the maxillary palp.

detailed.name: palpus
detailed.def: The anatomical cluster that is composed of the palpal segments.

detailed.name: paracoxal ridge
detailed.def: The ridge that extends along the anterior margin of the metapectus.

detailed.name: paracoxal sulcus
detailed.def: The sulcus that corresponds to the paracoxal ridge.

detailed.name: paraglossa
detailed.def: The lobe that is connected to the distal margin of the prementum posteroproximally, to the premental hypopharynx proximolaterally and anteroproximally, to the glossa proximomedially and bears the basiparaglossal brush and the paraglossal sclerite.

detailed.name: premento-paraglossal muscle
detailed.def: The labial muscle that arises from the ventral part of the prementum, anterior to the origin of the premento-glossal muscle and ventral premento-salivarial muscle and inserts just distally of the distal margin of the prementum.

detailed.name: paramandibular process
detailed.def: The projection that is located distolaterally on the hypostoma and extends to the clypeus separating the mandibular socket from the oral foramen.

detailed.name: paraocular area
detailed.def: The area that is located on the face and delimited ventromedially by the epistomal sulcus.

detailed.name: paraocular carina
detailed.def: The carina that surrounds the compound eye.

detailed.name: paraocular lobe
detailed.def: The area that is located ventromedially on the paraocular area and is surrounded partly by the epistomal sulcus.

detailed.name: parapenis
detailed.def: The area that is the dorsomedian part of the gonostipes and is the site of origin of the distodorsal and proximodorsal gonostyle/volsella complex-penisvalval muscles.

detailed.name: parapenisjugum
detailed.def: The area that is narrow and connects the parapenis with the rest of the gonostipes.

detailed.name: parapsidal line
detailed.def: The signum that is located between the notaulus and the parascutal carina and corresponds to the site of origin of the dorsoventral indirect flight muscle.

detailed.name: parapsis
detailed.def: The area that is delimited medially by the parapsidal line and laterally by the parascutal carina.

detailed.name: parascrobal area
detailed.def: The area that is located on the upper face, is delimited medially by the antennal scrobe and laterally by the inner orbit.

detailed.name: parascutal carina
detailed.def: The rim that extends along the lateral margin of the lateral area of the mesoscutum and delimits the preaxilla.

detailed.name: paravalva
detailed.def: The projection that is lateral and located apically on the penisvalva.

detailed.name: parietal
detailed.def: The area that is lateral on the cranium between the frontal and occipital areas, separated above by the coronal suture.

detailed.name: parietal band
detailed.def: The band that is located on the anterior part of the larval head.

detailed.name: parossiculo-penisvalval muscle
detailed.def: The male genitalia muscle that arises from the proximal apex of the parossiculus and inserts medially on the penisvalva. The muscle inserts on the ergot if present.

detailed.name: parossiculo-phallotremal muscle
detailed.def: The male genitalia muscle that originates from the parossiculus and inserts on the endophallic membrane around the phallotrema.

detailed.name: parossiculus
detailed.def: The sclerite that is connected via conjunctiva distomedially to the gonostipes, and articulates with the gonossiculus.

detailed.name: patch
detailed.def: The area that is round and differs from surrounding regions in sculpture, setae, and/or pigmentation.

detailed.name: pectus
detailed.def: The sclerite that is located ventrally of the thoracic segment and bears the furca and the lateral coxal condyle of the pleuron.

detailed.name: pedicel
detailed.def: The antennal segment that is connected proximally to the scape and distally to the flagellum.

detailed.name: penisvalva
detailed.def: The sclerite that is in the middle of the external male genitalia, surrounds the distal part of the ductus ejaculatorius and the endophallus.

synonyms.name: inner or dorsal arm of the stipes

detailed.name: penisvalvo-gonossiculal muscle
detailed.def: The gonossiculal muscle that arises from the penisvalva.

detailed.name: penisvalvo-phallotremal muscle
detailed.def: The male genitalia muscle that arises from the medial surface of the proximal part of the penisvalva and inserts on the endophallus just around the phallotrema.

detailed.name: peristome
detailed.def: The anatomical cluster that comprises the ventral marginal of the cranium, including the clypeus, the pleurostomata, and the hyperstomata.

detailed.name: peritreme
detailed.def: A sclerotic ring surrounding the outer spiracular opening.

detailed.name: phallobase
detailed.def: The anatomical cluster that is composed of the cupulae, gonostipites and volsellae.

detailed.name: phallotrema
detailed.def: The anatomical space that is the distal opening of the endophallus.

detailed.name: pharate adult
detailed.def: The developing adult after pupal-adult apolysis, i.e. from stage P8 (when yellow eye color first becomes visible through the pupal case), to eclosion.

detailed.name: phragma
detailed.def: The antecosta that corresponds to the site of attachment of the dorsal longitudinal muscles in the thorax.

detailed.name: pile
detailed.def: The anatomical cluster that is composed of fine, dense setae, acanthae or microtrichiae.

detailed.name: pit
detailed.def: The patch that is impressed and corresponds to an apophysis.

detailed.name: planta
detailed.def: The sclerite that is located proximoventrally on the pretarsus and is connected proximally with the unguitractor plate and distally with the arolium.

detailed.name: pleural apodeme
detailed.def: The apodeme that is located on the pleuron.

detailed.name: pleural apophysis
detailed.def: The apophysis that is adjacent to the pleural ridge.

detailed.name: pleural area
detailed.def: The anatomical cluster composed of the pro-, meso-, and metapleura.

detailed.name: pleural pit
detailed.def: The pit that is located on the pleuron and corresponds to the pleural apophysis.

detailed.name: pleural ridge
detailed.def: The ridge that extends between the pleural wing process and the lateral coxal articulation and marks the border between the episternum and epimeron.

detailed.name: pleural suture
detailed.def: The sulcus that corresponds to the pleural ridge and separates the episternum from the epimeron.

detailed.name: pleural wing process
detailed.def: The fossa that is located on the dorsal margin of the pleuron and accommodates the second axillary sclerite.

detailed.name: pleurite
detailed.def: The sclerite that is located on the pleuron.

detailed.name: pleuro-ventral line
detailed.def: The line of separation between the pleural region and the venter, lying mesad of the limb bases, but obscured when the latter are fused with the sterna.

detailed.name: pleuron
detailed.def: The sclerite that articulates with the leg and is connected along its dorsal margin with the notum and ventral margin with the sternum.

detailed.name: pleurostoma
detailed.def: The area that extends on the ventral (anterior) margin of the cranium along the site of origin of the conjunctiva connecting the mandible with the cranium and is delimited posteriorly (ventrally) by the posterior (ventral) margin of the pleurostomal fossa and anteriorly (dorsally) by the anterior (dorsal) margin of the pleurostomal condyle and laterally by the mouth margin.

detailed.name: pleurostomal condyle
detailed.def: The condyle that is located on the anterior (dorsal) margin of the pleurostoma and inserts into the mandibular acetabulum.

detailed.name: pleurostomal fossa
detailed.def: The fossa that is located postero- (ventro-) medially on the pleurostoma and accommodates the mandibular condyle.

detailed.name: pleurostomal ridge
detailed.def: The ridge that extends between the pleurostomal fossa and pleurostomal condyle and delimits the lateral margin of the pleurostoma.

detailed.name: pleurostomal suture
detailed.def: The suture that is dorsal to the mandible and is part of the subgenal suture.

detailed.name: plica
detailed.def: The carina that arises from the anterior margin of the abdominal tergum 1 medially of the propodeal spiracle extends to the posterior propodeal projection.

detailed.name: plica basalis
detailed.def: The wing fold that is located between the mediocubital field and axillary region.

detailed.name: plica vannalis
detailed.def: The wing fold that is located between the cubital field and the first vannal vein.

detailed.name: plical area
detailed.def: The area that is delimited laterally by the metapleural carina and medially by the plica.

detailed.name: pollex
detailed.def: The area corresponding to the upper margin of the mandible, above the acetabular groove, commonly ending in the preapical tooth or teeth.

detailed.name: postacetabular patch
detailed.def: The setiferous patch that is located ventrally on the mesopleuron posterior to the epicnemial carina.

detailed.name: postacetabular sulcus
detailed.def: The sulcus that extends posteriorly along the epicnemial carina.

detailed.name: postalar process
detailed.def: The process that is located anterolaterally on the mesoscutellar-axillar complex and separates the site of origin of the fore wing from the site of origin of the hind wing.

detailed.name: postclypeus
detailed.def: The area that is located on the clypeus and is delimited by the epistomal sulcus and a medial transverse anatomical line.

detailed.name: postcubitus
detailed.def: The usual sixth vein of the wing, associated basally with the cubitus.

detailed.name: anterior cranio-mandibular muscle
detailed.def: The mandibular muscle that arises posterodorsally from the cranium and inserts on the tendon attached anteroproximally on the mandible.

detailed.name: posterior extension of preaxilla
detailed.def: The area that is located on the posteroventral corner of the preaxilla and articulates with the anterior extension of the lateral axillar area.

detailed.name: posterior glossal plate
detailed.def: The area that is delimited laterally distally and proximally by the sclerites that are connected to the distal margin of the prementum and receive the site of insertion of the premento-paraglossal muscle.

detailed.name: posterior glossal sclerite
detailed.def: The sclerite of the posterior glossal plate that is paired.

detailed.name: posterior labral retractor
detailed.def: The labral muscle that arises from the upper face laterally to the anterior fronto-labral muscle and inserts on the torma.

detailed.name: posterior mesepimeral area
detailed.def: The area that extends along the posterior margin of the mesopleuron and is delimited anteriorly by the mesepimeral sulcus.

detailed.name: posterior mesepimeral inflection
detailed.def: The inflection that extends along the posterior margin of the mesepimeron, posteriorly of the subalar pit to the coxal articulation.

detailed.name: posterior mesofurco-mesolaterophragmal muscle
detailed.def: The mesolaterophragmal muscle that originates from the mesofurca and inserts on the mesolaterophragma posterior to the site of origin of the anterior mesofuco-mesolaterophragmal muscle.

detailed.name: posterior mesonoto-mesotrochanteral muscle
detailed.def: The mesonoto-mesotrochanteral muscle that arises from the dorsal axillar area, posteriorly of the site of origin of the anterior mesonoto-mesotrochanteral muscle (if present) and inserts on the mesotrochanteral apodeme.

detailed.name: posterior mesopleuro-mesofurcal muscle
detailed.def: The mesofurcal muscle that originates proximally from the mesofurca and inserts on the mesopleuron.

key: posterior mesopleuro-third axillary sclerite of fore wing muscle

detailed.name: posterior mesopleuro-third axillary sclerite of fore wing muscle
posterior mesoscutellar sulcus
detailed.def: The line that extends along the posterior margin of the mesoscutellum and corresponds to the posterior mesoscutellar ridge.

detailed.name: posterior notal wing process
detailed.def: The articular process that is located on the lateral margin of the alinotum and articulates with the first axillary sclerite.

detailed.name: posterior ocellar line
detailed.def: The anatomical line that is the shortest between the margin of the lateral ocelli.

detailed.name: posterior profurcal lamella
detailed.def: The ridge that extends along the dorsal margin of the dorsal profurcal lamella and corresponds to the site of origin of the lateral profurco-procoxal muscle.

detailed.name: posterior pronotal inflection
detailed.def: The inflection that extends along the posterior margin of the pronotum and articulates with the anterior mesopleural inflection.

detailed.name: posterior pronotal sulcus
detailed.def: The sulcus that extends parallel with the posterior margin of the pronotum and corresponds to the ridge of the posterior pronotal inflection.

detailed.name: posterior propodeal projection
detailed.def: The propodeal spine that is located posteromedially of the first abdominal spiracle.

detailed.name: posterior scutellar depression
detailed.def: The line that extends just posterior of the posterior mesoscutellar ridge/mesoscutellar arms and delimits the posterior margin of the mesoscutellar pulsatory organ and the anterior margin of the mesopostscutellum.

detailed.name: posterior scutum
detailed.def: The area that is located posteriorly on the mesoscutum.

detailed.name: posterior supraalar area
detailed.def: The area that is limited medially by the transmetanotal carina and bears the postmedian metanotal wing process.

detailed.name: posterior tentorial pit
detailed.def: The tentorial pit that corresponds to posterior tentorial arm.

detailed.name: posterior thoracic spiracle
detailed.def: The spiracle that is located dorsally on the border between the mesopectus and the metapectus.

detailed.name: posterodorsal edge of pronotum
detailed.def: The edge that is located at the intersection of the dorsal and lateral margins of the pronotum.

detailed.name: posterodorsal edge of the mesopleuron
detailed.def: The edge that is located at the intersection of the mesepimeral ridge and the inflected dorsal margin of the mesopleuron.

detailed.name: posterolateral pronotal area
detailed.def: The area that is limited anteriorly by the posterolateral pronotal sulcus.

detailed.name: posterolateral pronotal carina
detailed.def: The carina that extends parallel to the posterolateral margin of the pronotum and delimits the posterolateral margin of pronotum.

detailed.name: posterolateral pronotal sulcus
detailed.def: The sulcus that arises from the ventrolateral pit of the pronotum and reaches the posterolateral carina of the pronotum ventral to the anterior thoracic spiracle.

detailed.name: posteroventral metapleural pit
detailed.def: The pit that corresponds to the dilator muscle apodeme.

detailed.name: postgena
detailed.def: The area that is located on the occiput ventrally of the occipital foramen.

detailed.name: postgenal bridge
detailed.def: The subforaminal bridge that is sclerotised and limited anteriorly (ventrally) by the hypostomal carina and is subdivided medially by the median sulcus of the postgenal bridge.

detailed.name: postgenal cushion
detailed.def: The setiferous patch that is located on the postgena.

detailed.name: postgenal pit
detailed.def: The pit that corresponds to the ventral-most point of the posterior site of fusion of the ventral lamella of the tentorium.

detailed.name: postgenal sulcus
detailed.def: The sulcus that corresponds with the postgenal ridge.

detailed.name: postgenal suture
detailed.def: The groove that is median, vertical and extends between the occipital and oral foramina separating the postgenae.

synonyms.name: longitudinal sulci on ventral head sclerotization
median sulcus of the postgenal bridge

detailed.name: postmarginal vein
detailed.def: The abscissa that is marginal and located distal to the marginal vein.

detailed.name: postmedian notal wing process
detailed.def: The fossa that is located on the lateral axillar area and articulates with the posterior end of the first axillary sclerite of the fore wing.

detailed.name: postmentum
detailed.def: The anatomical cluster that is composed of the sclerites that are on the postmental area.

detailed.name: postnotum
detailed.def: The sclerite that bears the phragma and is located posterior to a notum.

detailed.name: postoccipital ridge
detailed.def: The ridge that corresponds to the postoccipital sulcus.

detailed.name: postoccipital sulcus
detailed.def: The sulcus that is located on the postoccipital suture.

detailed.name: postoccipital suture
detailed.def: The line that delimits the postocciput from the cranium.

detailed.name: postocciput
detailed.def: The area that is delimited by the postoccipital sulcus or postoccipital carina and the occipital foramen and bears the propleural fossa of the cranium.

detailed.name: postocellar furrow
detailed.def: The sulcus that extends between the posterior margin of the lateral ocelli.

detailed.name: postocellar vertex
detailed.def: The area posterior to the imaginary line intersecting the lateral ocelli and anterior to the occipital carina.

detailed.name: postorbital carina
detailed.def: The carina that extends posteriorly from the genal sulcus.

detailed.name: postpectal carina
detailed.def: The carina that delimits anterolaterally the mesocoxal depression.

synonyms.name: posterior transverse carina of the mesosternum

detailed.name: postphragma
detailed.def: The phragma that is located posteriorly on a thoracic segment.

detailed.name: poststernite
detailed.def: The area corresponding to the postcostal lip of a definitive sternal plate that includes the intersegmental sclerotization following.

detailed.name: posttergite
detailed.def: The area that corresponds to the narrow postcostal lip of a postnotal thoracic plate.

detailed.name: preapical tooth
detailed.def: The tooth that is located distodorsally on the mandible.

detailed.name: prearticular incisure
detailed.def: The notch that is located on the margin of the second valvifer immediately anterior to the basal articulation of the second valvulae.

detailed.name: preaxilla
detailed.def: The area that is delimited laterally by the parascutal carina, adjacent to the tegula anteriorly and bears the anterior and antemedian mesonotal wing processes.

detailed.name: preaxillar carina
detailed.def: The carina that extends across the preaxilla and separates the articulation area for the tegula.

detailed.name: postmento-premental muscle
detailed.def: The labial muscle that is unpaired, arises from the postmentum and inserts on the proximal margin of the prementum.

detailed.name: prementum
detailed.def: The sclerite that is median, is connected via conjunctiva along its proximolateral margins to the stipites, is articulated with the labial palps, is continuous along its distal margin with the ligula and distolateral margins with the distal hypopharynx and receives the site of attachments of the extrinsic labial palp muscles.

detailed.name: preoccipital carina
detailed.def: The carina that is transverse and extends posteriorly of the lateral ocelli and compound eye and delimits anteriorly the preoccipital lunula.

detailed.name: preoccipital furrow
detailed.def: The sulcus that arises medially from the anterior margin of the occipital carina and extends towards the anterior ocellus.

detailed.name: preoccipital lunula
detailed.def: The area that is concave, is located on the vertex and is limited anteriorly by the preoccipital carina and posteriorly by the occipital carina.

detailed.name: preocellar pit
detailed.def: The pit that is located on upper face adjacent the anterior margin of the median ocellus and corresponds internally to a conical apodeme and not the tentorium.

detailed.name: preoral cavity
detailed.def: The anatomical cluster that includes the labrum, hypopharynx and labium.

detailed.name: preorbital carina
detailed.def: The carina that is located on the face, parallels the inner orbit and is paired.

detailed.name: prepectus
detailed.def: The intersegmentalia that is located on the mesopectus-pronotum intersegmental membrane and corresponds to the site of origin of the anterior thoracic spiracle occlusor muscle.

detailed.name: prescutal ridge
detailed.def: The ridge that seperates the prescutum from scutum.

detailed.name: prescutal sulcus
detailed.def: The sulcus that sets off the prescutum from the scutum and corresponds to the prescutal ridge.

detailed.name: prescutum
detailed.def: The area that is located on the alinotum and is limited by the antecostal suture and prescutal suture.

detailed.name: prespecular sulcus
detailed.def: The sulcus that delimits anteriorly the speculum and corresponds to the anterior margin of the speculum.

detailed.name: prespiracular propodeal area
detailed.def: The area that is located between the first metasomal spiracle and metapleural arm and delimited laterally by the metapleural carina.

detailed.name: presternum
detailed.def: The area that is located on the basisternum and is delimited by the sternacostal sulcus.

detailed.name: prestigma
detailed.def: The patch that is enlarged, sclerotized, and located at the apex of one or more wing veins on the anterior margin of the wing.

detailed.name: pretarsus
detailed.def: The anatomical cluster that is apical to the telotarsus and composed of the empodium, auxilia, planta, pulvillum, unguis, unguitractor plate, auxiliar sclerite and manubrium.

detailed.name: primary gonopore
detailed.def: The anatomical space that is the transition from the ductus ejaculatorius to the endophallus and therefore the transition from the internal to the external male genitalia.

detailed.name: process
detailed.def: The area on the sclerite that is raised.

detailed.name: prodiscrimen
detailed.def: The discrimen that is located in the prothorax and corresponds to the prodiscrimenal lamella.

detailed.name: prodiscrimenal lamella
detailed.def: The discrimenal lamella that is located in the prothorax.

detailed.name: profurca
detailed.def: The anatomical cluster that is composed of the profurcal arms.

detailed.name: profurcal arm
detailed.def: The sternal apophysis that is paired, arises from the furcasternum of the prothorax and continuous with the prodiscrimenal lamella.

detailed.name: profurcal pit
detailed.def: The furcal pit that is located in the prothorax.

detailed.name: profurco-laterocervical muscle
detailed.def: The laterocervical muscle that arises medially from the anterior surface of the dorsal profurcal lamella.

detailed.name: projection
detailed.def: The process that is located on an edge.

detailed.name: pronope
detailed.def: The depression that is dorsomedian and is located on the pronotum.

detailed.name: pronotal cervical sulcus
detailed.comment: The class is equivalent with the transverse pronotal sulcus.

detailed.name: pronotal collar
detailed.def: The area of the pronotum that is subhorizontal and located posteriorly of the pronotal neck.

detailed.name: pronotal cushion
detailed.def: The setiferous patch that corresponds with the transverse sulcus of the pronotum.

detailed.name: pronotal flange
detailed.def: The evagination that extends along the anterior margin of the pronotum and overlaps the head-propectus articulation.

detailed.name: pronotal lobe
detailed.def: The area that is located dorsally on the posterior margin of the pronotum and overlaps the anterior thoracic spiracle.

detailed.name: pronotal neck
detailed.def: The area of the pronotum that is delimited posterodorsally by an edge and that accommodates the posterior surface of the head.

detailed.name: pronotal plate
detailed.def: The area of the pronotum that is median, raised and limited laterally by sharp edges.

detailed.name: pronotal projection
detailed.def: The projection that is part of the pronotum.

detailed.name: pronotal shoulder
detailed.def: The corner that is located anterolaterally on the horizontal part of the collar.

detailed.name: pronotal side
detailed.def: The area that is located on the pronotum and that is vertical.

detailed.name: pronotal suprahumeral sulcus
detailed.def: The sulcus that extends along the dorsal margin of the pronotum and delimits the dorsal pronotal area.

detailed.name: pronotal verricule
detailed.def: The verricule that is located on the anterosubmedial pronotum.

detailed.name: pronoto-laterocervical muscle
detailed.def: The laterocervical muscle that arises medially from the pronotum and insert on the cervical apodeme.

detailed.name: pronoto-mesobasalar muscle
detailed.def: The mesobasalar muscle that originates from the posterolateral part of the pronotum and inserts on the mesobasalare.

detailed.name: pronoto-anterior thoracic spiracle muscle
detailed.def: The anterior thoracic spiracle occlusor muscle that arises from the pronotum.

detailed.name: pronoto-postoccipital muscle
detailed.def: The postoccipital muscle that arises from the pronotum and inserts on the postocciput.

detailed.name: pronoto-procoxal muscle
detailed.def: The procoxal muscle that arises from the pronotum and inserts on the posterolateral margin of the procoxa.

detailed.name: pronoto-profurcal muscle
detailed.def: The profurcal muscle that arises along the ventral margin of the pronotum, and inserts laterally on the profurca.

detailed.name: pronoto-prophragmal muscle
detailed.def: The prophragmal muscle that originates medially from the pronotum and inserts submedially on the prophragma.

detailed.name: pronoto-propleural muscle
detailed.def: The propleural muscle that arises medially from the pronotum and inserts on the posterodorsal margin of the propleuron.

detailed.name: pronotum
detailed.def: The notum that is located in the prothorax.

detailed.name: propectus
detailed.def: The anatomical cluster that is composed of the prosternum and the propleuron.

detailed.name: prophragma
detailed.def: The phragma that extends along the anterior margin of the mesonotum.

detailed.name: propleural arm
detailed.def: The apodeme that is located on the posterodorsal corner of the lateral propleural area and corresponds to the site of muscle attachment.

detailed.name: propleural cervical sulcus
detailed.def: The sulcus that extends along the dorsal margin of the propleuron.

detailed.name: propleural epicoxal sulcus
detailed.def: The epicoxal sulcus that sets of the epicoxal lobe from the ventral part of the ventral propleural area.

detailed.name: propleuro-postoccipital muscle
detailed.def: The postoccipital muscle that arises from the propleuron and inserts on the postocciput.

detailed.name: propleuro-procoxal muscle
detailed.def: The procoxal muscle that arises from along the posterodorsal margin of the propleuron and inserts on the procoxa.

detailed.name: propleuro-protrochanteral muscle
detailed.def: The protrochanteral muscle that arises from the propleuron and inserts on the protrochanteral apodeme.

detailed.name: propleuron
detailed.def: The pleuron that is articulated with the fore leg, connected dorsolaterally (anterolaterally) with the pronotum and ventrally (posteriorly) with the prosternum.

detailed.name: propodeal bridge
detailed.def: The area that is located between the propodeal foramen and metacoxal foramina.

detailed.name: propodeal carina
detailed.def: The carina that is located on the propodeum.

detailed.name: propodeal foramen
detailed.def: The foramen that is located on the metapectal-propodeal complex in which the petiole is positioned.

detailed.name: propodeal tooth
detailed.def: The articular process that is located on the margin of the propodeal-metapectal complex around the propodeal foramen and bears the metasomal condyle of the propodeum.

detailed.name: propodeo-second abdominal sternal muscle
detailed.def: The second abdominal sternal muscle that originates ventral to the metaphragma and inserts on the anterodorsal margin of the abdominal sternite 2.

detailed.name: propodeo-second abdominal tergal muscle
detailed.def: The second abdominal tergal muscle that arises from the abdominal tergum 1 and inserts on the abdominal tergum 2.

detailed.name: prospinasterno-mesofurcal muscle
detailed.def: The mesofurcal muscle that arises from the prospinasternum and inserts on the mesofurca.

detailed.name: prosternal incision
detailed.def: The notch that separates the basisternum from the furcasternum in the prosternum.

detailed.name: prosterno-procoxal muscle
detailed.def: The procoxal muscle that arises from the prosternum and inserts on the procoxa.

detailed.name: prosternum
detailed.def: The sclerite that is connected posteriorly to the propleuron and bears the prodiscrimen and the profurca.

detailed.name: prothorax
detailed.def: The thoracic segment that is located between the head and the mesothorax is composed of the pronotum, the propectus and the fore leg.

detailed.name: protibial spur
detailed.def: The tibial spur that is located on the fore leg, is curved and together with the probasitarsus forms the strigil.

detailed.name: proximal gonostipo-parossiculal muscle
detailed.def: The gonostyle-parossiculal muscle that arises proximally from the lateral part of the gonostipes and inserts on the proximal part of the parossiculus.

detailed.name: proximodorsal gonostyle/volsella complex-penisvalval muscle
detailed.def: The dorsal gonostyle/volsella complex penisvalval muscle that arises proximodorsally from the gonostyle/volsella complex and inserts on the penisvalva distally of the site of insertion of the distodorsal gononstyle/volsella complex-penisvalva muscle.

detailed.name: proximolateral apodeme of cupula
detailed.def: The apodeme that is located laterally on the proximal margin of the cupula and corresponds to the site of insertion of the second lateral 9th sterno-cupulal muscle.

detailed.name: proximoventral gonostyle/volsella complex-penisvalval muscle
detailed.def: The ventral gonostyle/volsella complex-penisvalval muscle that arises ventromedially from the gonostyle/volsella complex, inserts on the proximal end of the penisvalva and is oriented proximodorsally.

detailed.name: pseudoceps
detailed.def: The projection that is medial and located apically on the penisvalva.

detailed.name: pseudophragma
detailed.def: The flange that extends along the dorsal margin of the dorsal mesopostnotal flange.

detailed.name: pteralia
detailed.def: The anatomical cluster that is composed of the axillary sclerites and the humeral plate.

detailed.name: pterothorax
detailed.def: The anatomical cluster that is composed of the meso- and metathorax and if the latter is fused with, the first abdominal tergum.

detailed.name: puncture
detailed.def: The depression that is point-like, without a flat bottom, and occurs in areas of punctate surface sculpture.

detailed.name: pygidial plate
detailed.def: The area that is located posteriorly on the tergum of the last externally visible segment of the abdomen.

detailed.name: radial vein
detailed.def: The wing vein that is connected to the second axillary sclerite and extends posterior to the subcosta.

detailed.name: radicle
detailed.def: The area that is located proximally on the scape, is limited distally by a constriction and bears proximally the basal knob.

detailed.name: radio-medial cross vein
detailed.def: The wing vein that connects the radial vein with the medial vein.

detailed.name: ramal rod
detailed.def: The area that extends external to the dorsal ramus of the first valvula.

synonyms.name: ventral ramus of the first valvula

detailed.name: rami valvularum
detailed.def: The area that is located proximally on valvulae and is connected to the valvifers.

detailed.name: remigial region
detailed.def: The wing region anterior to the vannal fold containing the costa, subcosta, radius, medius, cubitus, and postcubitus.

detailed.name: reproductive system
detailed.def: The anatomical system that is involved in reproduction.

detailed.name: retractor of the epipharynx
detailed.def: The epipharyngeal muscle that arises from the clypeus and inserts on the epipharyngeal wall.

synonyms.name: retractor, or levator, of the epipharynx

detailed.name: retractor of the mouth angle
detailed.def: The hypopharyngeal muscle that arises from the upper face and inserts on the proximolateral process of the sitophore.

detailed.name: rhachis
detailed.def: The ridge that extends along the ventral surface of the second valvula that is partially enclosed by the aulax.

detailed.name: ridge
detailed.def: The apodeme that is elongate.

detailed.name: rim
detailed.def: The carina that extends along the margin or edge of a sclerite.

detailed.name: row
detailed.def: The anatomical cluster that is composed of repeated units of anatomical structures.

detailed.name: row of impressions
detailed.def: The row that is composed of impressions.

detailed.name: row of setae
detailed.def: The row that is composed of setae.

detailed.name: ruga
detailed.def: The carina that is irregularly non-linear.

detailed.name: salivarial orifice
detailed.def: The anatomical space that is situated posteriorly in the salivarium, proximal to the insertion of the dilatators.

detailed.name: salivarium
detailed.def: The area that is adjacent to the proximal end of the salivary duct and corresponds to the site of insertion of the dorsal and ventral premento-salivarial muscles.

detailed.name: scale
detailed.def: The sensillum with a scale-like cuticular component.

detailed.name: scape
detailed.def: The antennal segment that is proximal to the pedicel and is connected to the head via the radicle.

detailed.name: sclerite
detailed.def: The area of the cuticle that is less flexible than adjacent conjunctivae.

detailed.name: scopa
detailed.def: The setiferous patch that is used for pollen storage.

detailed.name: scrobal depression
detailed.def: The anatomical cluster that is composed of antennal scrobes.

detailed.name: scrobe
detailed.def: The area that is impressed and is for the reception or concealment of another sclerite.

detailed.name: sculpture
detailed.def: The area that is located on the sclerite and that is composed of repetitive anatomical structures.

detailed.name: scutellar bridge
detailed.def: The apodeme that originates from the scutoscutellar ridge and fuses with the posterior part of the mesoscutellum.

detailed.name: scutellar disc
detailed.def: The area that is median, convex and is delimited anteriorly by the scutoscutellar sulcus and laterally by the axillar depression.

detailed.name: scutellar fovea
detailed.def: The depression that is medially located on the scutoscutellar suture.

detailed.name: scutoscutellar ridge
detailed.def: The ridge that extends along the anterior margin of the mesoscutellum and corresponds to the scutoscutellar suture.

detailed.name: scutoscutellar sulcus
detailed.def: The sulcus that extends along the scutoscutellar suture.

detailed.name: scutoscutellar suture
detailed.def: The line that traverses the mesonotum between the postmedian notal wing processes.

detailed.name: second axillary sclerite
detailed.def: The axillary sclerite that articulates ventrally with the pleuron and medially with the first axillary sclerite, posteriorly with the third axillary sclerite and is connected with the base of the radial vein.

detailed.name: second median plate
detailed.def: The sclerite that is located at the base of the mediocubital field, folding convexly on the outer edge of the first median plate along the plica basalis.

detailed.name: second mesopleuro-mesonotal muscle
detailed.def: The mesonotal muscle that arises medially from mesopleuron and inserts on the ventral surface of the lateral axillar area.

detailed.name: second phragmo-third phragmal muscle
detailed.def: The metaphragmal muscle that arises from the mesophragma and inserts on the metaphragma.

detailed.name: proximal gonostyle/volsella complex-harpal muscle
detailed.def: The gonostyle/volsella complex-harpal muscle that inserts on the median wall of the harpe and arises proximally of the site of origin of the distal gonostyle/volsella complex-harpal muscle.

detailed.name: second valvifer
detailed.def: The area of the second valvifer-second valvula-third valvula complex that is proximal to the basal articulation and to the processus musculares and articulates with female T9.

detailed.name: second valvula
detailed.def: The area of the second valvifer-second valvula-third valvula complex that is distal to the basal articulation and to the processus musculares and is limited medially by the median body axis.

detailed.name: segment
detailed.def: An anatomical structure that is metameric and is connected to other metameric subdivisions by muscles and is delimited by its sclerites.

detailed.name: sense organ
detailed.def: Multicellular anatomical structure with largely bona fide boundary that transduces some sensory stimulus to the nervous system.

detailed.name: sensillar patch
detailed.def: The patch that differs from surrounding region by presence of sensilla.

key: sensillar patch of the male flagellomere

detailed.name: sensillar patch of the male flagellomere
detailed.def: The sensillar patch that is located on the ventral surface of the male flagellomere.

detailed.name: sensillum
detailed.def: A sense organ embedded in the integument and consisting of one or a cluster of sensory neurons and associated sensory structures, support cells and glial cells forming a single organized unit with a largely bona fide boundary.

detailed.name: sensillum trichodeum curvatum
detailed.def: The sensillum that is curved.

detailed.name: seta
detailed.comment: This class, based on its primary definition, is equivalent with HAO:0000933 (mx:865). Uri tables that used this class refer 0002299.

detailed.name: setiferous patch
detailed.def: The patch that differs from the surrounding region by having denser setae.

detailed.name: sex flagellomere
detailed.def: The flagellomere that is modified and located on a male organism.

detailed.name: signum
detailed.def: The area that corresponds to the site of the origin of a muscle.

detailed.name: sitophore
detailed.def: The sclerite that is located in the proximal part of the hypopharygeal wall delimited distally by the functional mouth and proximally by the proximal boundary of the cibarium. The tentorio-hypopharyngeal muscle inserts on the proximal margin of the sitophore.

detailed.name: skaphion
detailed.def: The area that is anteriorly on the mesonotum and delimited posteriorly by the skaphion carina.

detailed.name: skaphion carina
detailed.def: The carina that delimits the skaphion posteriorly.

detailed.name: spatha
detailed.def: The sclerite that is unpaired and located just dorsally of the basal part of the aedeagus in some Aculeata.

detailed.name: specialized adhesive spur
detailed.def: The spur that is located on the tibia and is adhesive.

detailed.name: speculum
detailed.def: The area that is located dorsolaterally on the mesosoma, is delimited posteriorly by the mesepimeral ridge and corresponds to the site of origin of the anterior mesopleuro-mesofurcal muscle.

detailed.name: spermatheca
detailed.def: The invagination just proximal to the vagina that accommodates sperms.

detailed.name: spiculum
detailed.def: The apophysis that is located medially on the anterior margin of the abdominal sternum 9 and corresponds to the site of origin of the mediolateral S9-cupulal muscles.

synonyms.name: basal apodeme of ninth abdominal sternum

detailed.name: spina
detailed.def: The apophysis that is located medially on the spinasternum.

detailed.name: spinasternum
detailed.def: The sclerite that is located anteriorly on the thoracic sternum and bears the spina.

detailed.name: spine
detailed.def: The process that lacks non-sclerotised ring at the base.

detailed.name: spiracle
detailed.def: The anatomical cluster that is composed of the distal end of the trachea and the margin of the sclerite or conjunctiva surrounding the spiracular opening.

detailed.name: spur
detailed.def: The process that is surrounded by conjunctiva and evaginated and that is basally sclerotized.

detailed.name: sternacostal suture
detailed.def: The suture that extends along the border of the basisternum and sternellum.

detailed.name: sternaulus
detailed.def: The line that marks the site of origin of the mesopleuro-basalare and first mesopleuro-mesonotal muscle.

detailed.name: sternellum
detailed.def: The area that is located on the eusternum posteriorly of the site of origin of the furca.

detailed.name: sternite
detailed.def: The sclerite that is located on the sternum.

detailed.name: sternum
detailed.def: The area that is located on the integument and is ventral of the ventral diaphragm.

detailed.name: stigma
detailed.def: The patch on the wing that is sclerotized and is on the anterior margin of the fore wing.

detailed.name: stipes
detailed.def: The appendage that is connected posteroproximally to the hypostoma, anteroproximally and lateroproximally to the mandible and medioproximally to the labium and the hypopharynx via conjunctiva, is connected to the cranium via muscles and that bears the maxillary palp.

detailed.name: subalar area
detailed.def: The area that is delimited by the mesepimeral inflection and corresponding subalar ridge and bears the mesopleural wing process.

detailed.name: subalar pit
detailed.def: The pit that is located posterodorsally on the mesopectus corresponds to the posterodorsal edge of the mesopleuron.

detailed.name: subalare
detailed.def: The sclerite that is located on the dorsal margin of the pterothoracic pectus posterior to the pleural wing articulation.

detailed.name: hypoproct
detailed.def: The area that is located in between the anal opening and the genitalia.

detailed.name: subantennal area
detailed.def: The area that is located ventrally of the antennal rim and is delimited by the subantennal grooves and the epistomal sulcus.

detailed.name: subantennal groove
detailed.def: The impression that is located on the face ventrally of the antennal foramen and dorsally of the clypeus.

detailed.name: subaxillular bar
detailed.def: The area that is elevated, located on the lateroventral margin of the mesoscutellar axillar complex and is continuous anteriorly with the postalar process.

detailed.name: subcosta
detailed.def: The wing vein that is connected to the first axillary sclerite and extends posterior to the costa.

detailed.name: subgenal carina
detailed.def: The carina that is located on the cranium, ventrally of the gena and extending from the hypostomal carina to the facial articulation of the mandible.

detailed.name: subgenal coronet
detailed.def: The row of setae that extends along the carina paramandibular process and curves behind the mandibular base.

detailed.name: submarginal carina of abdominal tergite
detailed.def: The carina that extends laterally on the abdominal tergite.

detailed.name: submarginal groove
detailed.def: The impression extends along the lateral margin of the metasomal mediotergite.

detailed.name: submarginal vein
detailed.def: Basal-most portion of the forewing vein complex that occurs behind the costal cell; measured from the constriction that delimits the humeral plate to the point at which the vein touches the leading edge of the wing apically.

detailed.name: submedian carina
detailed.def: The carina that arises from the anterior mandibular articulation that is located medially of the orbital carina and laterally of the frontal carina.

detailed.name: submetapleural carina
detailed.def: The carina that is located along the ventral metapleural margin, between the mesocoxal foramen and metacoxal foramen.

detailed.name: subpleural signum
detailed.def: The signum that is located submedially on the ventral face of the mesopleuron. The subpleural signum corresponds to the site of origin of the first mesopleuro-mesonotal (pl2-t2a) muscle.

detailed.name: subpleuron
detailed.def: The area that is located ventrally on the pectus.

detailed.name: sulcus
detailed.def: The groove that corresponds to a ridge.

detailed.name: supraalar area
detailed.def: The area that is located laterally of the longitudinal metanotal carina.

detailed.name: epiproct
detailed.def: The sclerite that is located dorsally of the anal opening.

detailed.name: supraclypeal area
detailed.def: The area that is located medially on the lower face between the dorsal margin of the clypeus and the anatomical line that connects the dorsalmost points of the left and right antennal rims.

detailed.name: suture
detailed.def: The line that marks the fusion of two ancestral sclerites.

detailed.name: synsternal setiferous patch
detailed.def: The setiferous patch that is located on the synsternum.

detailed.name: synsternal translucent patch
detailed.def: The translucent patch that is located on the synsternite.

detailed.name: synsternum
detailed.def: The sternite that is at least two times as long as an adjacent sternite.

detailed.name: syntergal translucent patch
detailed.def: The translucent patch that is located submedially on the syntergum.

detailed.name: syntergum
detailed.def: The sclerite that is composed of fused abdominal terga.

detailed.name: tagma
detailed.def: The anatomical structure that is a distinctly delimited group of segments.

detailed.name: tarsal claw
detailed.def: The spur that is curved and projects from the apex of the last tarsal segment on either side of the arolium of the pretarsus.

detailed.name: tarsal plantula
detailed.def: The projection that is median, membraneous and is located distally on the ventral margin of the tarsomere.

detailed.name: tarsomere
detailed.def: The annulus that is the part of the tarsus.

detailed.name: tarsus
detailed.def: The leg segment that is apical to the tibia.

detailed.name: fore wing tegula
detailed.def: The cuticular evagination that is located laterally of the preaxilla and obscures the anterior mesonoto-first axillary articulation and the mesopleuro-second axillary sclerite joints.

detailed.name: telotarsus
detailed.def: The tarsomere that is apical.

detailed.name: temple
detailed.def: The area that is located on the gena, posterior to the compound eye.

detailed.name: tendon
detailed.def: The cuticular invagination on which a muscle is attached.

detailed.name: tentorial arm
detailed.def: The apodeme that extends between the posterior tentorial pit and the anterior tentorial pit.

detailed.name: tentorial bridge
detailed.def: The apodeme that connects medially the tentorial arms.

detailed.name: tentorial pit
detailed.def: The pit on the cranium that corresponds to the tentorium.

detailed.name: tentorio-antennal muscle
detailed.def: The muscle that arises from the dorsal surface of the anterior broadened part of the anterior tentorial arm and inserts on the scape.

detailed.name: tentorio-labial muscle
detailed.def: The labial muscle that arises from the cranium and inserts on the labium.

detailed.name: tentorio-stipital muscle
detailed.def: The maxillar muscle that arises on the posteroventral part of the anterior tentorial arm and inserts on the median wall of the stipes.

detailed.name: tentorium
detailed.def: The apodeme that has its sites of origin marked by the anterior and posterior tentorial pits and gular sulci.

detailed.name: terebra
detailed.def: The anatomical cluster that is composed of the first and second valvulae.

detailed.name: tergite
detailed.def: The sclerite that is located on the tergum.

detailed.name: tergum
detailed.def: The area that is located on the integument and is dorsal of the ventral diaphragm.

detailed.name: testis
detailed.def: The gonad that is consisting of testis follicles, is connected with the vas deferens and seminal vesicle.

detailed.name: third abdominal tergite
detailed.def: The tergite that is located in the third abdominal segment.

detailed.name: third axillary
detailed.def: The axillary sclerite that corresponds to the site of insertion of the mesopleuro-third axillary sclerite muscles and is connected proximally with the fourth axillary sclerite.

detailed.name: third mesopleuro-mesonotal muscle
detailed.def: The mesonotal muscle that originates from the ventral apodeme of the first phragma and inserts on the acropleural apodeme of the mesopleuron.

detailed.name: third phragmo-second abdominal tergal muscle
detailed.def: The second abdominal tergal muscle that arises from the metaphragma and inserts on the dorsal surface of the second abdominal tergum.

detailed.name: third valvula
detailed.def: The area of the second valvifer-third valvula complex that is posterior to the distal vertical conjunctiva of the second valvifer-third valvula complex.

synonyms.name: valves de la gaine du gorgeret

detailed.name: thoracic segment
detailed.def: The body segment that is located in the thorax.

detailed.name: thoracic sternum
detailed.def: The sternum that is located on a thoracic segment.

detailed.name: thorax
detailed.def: The tagma that is composed of the prothorax, mesothorax and metathorax.

detailed.name: thyridium
detailed.def: The patch that is located on the gastrocoelus and has specialized sculpture.

detailed.name: tibia
detailed.def: The leg segment that is proximal to the tarsus and distal to the femur.

detailed.name: tibial spur
detailed.def: The spur that is located distally on a tibia.

detailed.name: tooth
detailed.def: The projection that is located distally on the mandible.

detailed.name: torular space
detailed.def: The area that is located between the antennal rims.

detailed.name: torular triangle
detailed.def: The area that is delimited by the ventrally bifurcating central keel.

detailed.name: torulus
detailed.def: The foramen that is located on the head in which the radicle is positioned.

detailed.name: transaxillar carina
detailed.def: The carina that divides the dorsal axillar area into horizontal and vertical areas.

detailed.name: translucent patch
detailed.def: The patch that is less melanized than the surrounding area and is translucent.

detailed.name: transmetanotal carina
detailed.def: The carina that delimits the supraalar area posteriorly.

detailed.name: transpleural sulcus
detailed.def: The sulcus that extends from the mesopleural pit to the mesocoxal articulation.

detailed.name: transscutal fissure
detailed.def: The line that traverses the mesoscutum and originates from the lateral margin between the antemedian and postmedian notal wing processes.

detailed.name: transverse carina on petiole
detailed.def: The carina that delimits the anterior vertical area of abdominal segment 2.

detailed.name: transverse epipharyngeal muscle
detailed.def: The epipharyngeal muscle that extends between the lateral parts of the sitophore.

detailed.name: transverse pronotal carina
detailed.def: The carina that delimits posteriorly the pronotal neck.

detailed.name: transverse pronotal sulcus
detailed.def: The sulcus that corresponds to the anteromedian pronotal ridge.

detailed.name: trochantellus
detailed.def: The area that is located proximally on the femur and is delimited by a groove.

detailed.name: trochanter
detailed.def: The leg segment that is located proximal to the femur and distal to the coxa.

detailed.name: trochantin
detailed.def: The sclerite that is located anterolaterally of the coxa and corresponds to the site of insertion of the notal-trochantinal muscle.

detailed.name: tubercle
detailed.def: The process that is small and knob-like or round.

detailed.name: tuberculate verricule
detailed.def: The verricule that arises from a tubercle.

detailed.name: type I sensillum trichodea
detailed.def: Sensilla trichodea type I occur on the distal ends [...] of the antennal flagellum.  They have small conical basal sockets with slender and strongly bent shafts.

detailed.name: mechanosensory hair
detailed.def: The aporous sensillum that has a hairlike cuticular component.

detailed.name: umbilicated fovea
detailed.def: The depression that is medially raised and bears medially a seta.

detailed.name: uncus
detailed.def: The abscissa that extends anteriorly from the stigmal vein.

detailed.name: unguifer
detailed.def: The area that is located distomedially on the end of the telotarsus and articulates with pretarsal claws.

detailed.name: unguitractor plate
detailed.def: The sclerite that corresponds to the site of insertion of the flexor of the pretarsus.

detailed.name: upper face
detailed.def: The area that is located dorsally of the ventral margin of the antennal rim and ventrally of the anterior ocellus medial to the inner margins of the eye and malar line.

detailed.name: upper mesopleural rim
detailed.def: The carina that traverses the subdorsal mesopleuron.

detailed.name: valvar ridge
detailed.def: The ridge that is vertical and extends along the valviceps.

detailed.name: valviceps
detailed.def: The area that is the distal part of the penisvalva dorsally of the ergot.

detailed.name: valvispina
detailed.def: The spine that is located on the paravalva.

detailed.name: valvura
detailed.def: The area that is located proximally of the ergot on the penisvalva.

detailed.name: vannal region
detailed.def: The area that is delimited by the vannal veins, or veins that articulates with the third axillary sclerite.

detailed.name: vas deferens
detailed.def: The duct that connect the testis with the ejaculatory duct.

detailed.name: velum
detailed.def: The area that is lamella-like and is located on the calcar.

key: ventral apodeme of the first phragma

detailed.name: ventral apodeme of the first phragma
detailed.def: The apodeme that is located anterolaterally on the prophragma and corresponds to the site of origin of the third mesopleuro-mesonotal muscle.

detailed.name: ventral bridge of the pronotum
detailed.def: The area that connects ventrally the left and right halves of the pronotum.

detailed.name: ventral carina of the metapleuron
detailed.def: The carina that is located on the border of the metepisternal depression and the metacoxal depression.

detailed.name: ventral cibarial dilatator
detailed.def: The hypopharyngeal muscle that is unpaired and arises from the median anterior part of the tentorial bridge and inserts on the proximal part of the ventral wall of the cibarium or on the proximal margin of the sitophore, immediately distally to the point where the alimentary canal passes between the lateral arms of the sitophore.

detailed.name: ventral edge of propleuron
detailed.def: The edge that sets off the ventral vertical lobe of the propleuron from the ventral propleural area.

detailed.name: ventral lamella of the tentorium
detailed.def: The ridge that extends ventrally on the posterior part of the tentorial arm.

detailed.name: ventral mesopostnotal flange
detailed.def: The area that is more heavily sclerotised than surrounding areas, traverse the mesopostnotum, unites laterally with the dorsal mesopostnotal flange, continuous laterally with the laterophragma and ventrally (posteriorly) with the mesophragma.

detailed.name: ventral metafurcal lamella
detailed.def: The ridge that is located ventrally on the metafurcal arm.

detailed.name: ventral metapleural area
detailed.def: The area that is located on the metapleuron anteroventrally of the metapleural sulcus.

detailed.name: ventral pharyngeal dilatator
detailed.def: The pharyngeal muscle that arises from the dorsal surface of the tentorial bridge and inserts ventrally on the pharyngeal wall.

detailed.name: anterior tentorio-premental muscle
detailed.def: The muscle that arises anteriorly from the cranium and inserts on the posterior margin of the prementum.

detailed.name: ventral profurcal lamella
detailed.def: The ridge that is located ventrally on the profurcal arm and corresponds to the site of origin of the profurco-procoxal muscle.

detailed.name: ventral profurco-postoccipital muscle
detailed.def: The postoccipital muscle that arises partly from the anterior surface of the dorsal profurcal lamella ventral to the site of origin of the dorsal profurco-postoccipital muscle and partly from the median area of the dorsal profurcal lamella.

detailed.name: ventral pronotal pit
detailed.def: The pit that is located ventrally on the pronotum.

detailed.name: ventral propleural area
detailed.def: The area that is located ventrally on the propleuron and is delimited laterally by the longitudinal carina of the propleuron.

detailed.name: ventral propodeal carina
detailed.def: The carina that separates the metasomal depression from the metacoxal depression.

detailed.name: ventral ridge of clypeus
detailed.def: The ridge that arises medially from the pleurostomal condyle and corresponds to the site of attachment of the labrum.

detailed.name: ventral row of spines
detailed.def: The row that is composed of spines and is located ventrally on the apex of the calcar.

detailed.name: ventral salivarial dilatator
detailed.def: The salivarial muscle that arises from the proximal end of the ventral part of the prementum and inserts on the salivarial sclerite.

key: ventral vertical lobe of the propleuron

detailed.name: ventral vertical lobe of the propleuron
detailed.def: The area that is limited dorsally (anteriorly) by the ventral edge of the propleuron.

detailed.name: ventrolateral cupolo-gonostyle/volsella complex
detailed.def: The cupulo-gonostyle/volsella complex muscle that inserts ventrolaterally on the gonostyle/volsella complex between the site of insertion of the ventromedial and dorsolateral cupulo-gonostyle/volsella complex muscles.

detailed.name: ventromedial cupulo-gonostyle/volsella complex muscle
detailed.def: The cupulo-gonostyle/volsella complex muscle that inserts medially on the ventral region of the gonostyle/volsella complex.

detailed.name: verricule
detailed.def: The setiferous patch that is composed of closely gathered setae.

detailed.name: vertex
detailed.def: The area that is delimited by the intersection of the margin of the compound eyes, the interorbital plane, and the anatomical line that is tangential to the point on the margin of the anterior ocellus which defines the minimum distance between the anterior ocellus and the oral foramen.

detailed.name: vertex patch
detailed.def: The setiferous patch that is located on the vertex.

key: vertical apodemal lobe of the mesoscutellum

detailed.name: vertical apodemal lobe of the mesoscutellum
detailed.def: The inflection that extends along the posterolateral margin of the mesoscutellum and articulates with the dorsal propodeal inflection.

detailed.name: vertical lobe of mesoscutum
detailed.def: The inflection that extends ventrally along the anterolateral margin of the mesoscutum.

detailed.name: vesicula seminalis
detailed.def: The anatomical space that functions as storage of spermatozoa.

detailed.name: vestibulum
detailed.def: The anatomical space that is located dorsally of abdominal sternum 7 when the latter extends beyond the abdominal sternum 8.

detailed.name: villus
detailed.def: The area that is covered with dense setae and is located posterior to the metapleural spiracle.

detailed.name: volsella
detailed.def: The anatomical cluster that is composed of the sclerites on the ventral part of the male genitalia that are not connected to the cupula via muscles.

detailed.name: volsellar plate
detailed.def: The area that is located on the parossiculus ventromedially of the cuspis.

detailed.name: volsellar ridge
detailed.def: The ridge that is located on the parossiculus, originates from the gonossiculo-parossicular articulation and extends towards the proximal margin of the parossiculus.

detailed.name: vulva
detailed.def: The anatomical space that corresponds to the opening of the vagina.

detailed.name: Waterston's evaporatorium
detailed.def: The evaporatorium that is located medially on the acrotergite of abdominal tergite 7.

detailed.name: wing
detailed.def: The appendage that is between the notum and the pectus and is connected to the body by the axillary sclerite muscles.

detailed.name: wing base
detailed.def: The anatomical cluster that is composed of the pteralia, humeral complex, tegula and axillary cord.

detailed.name: wing cell
detailed.def: The wing membrane that is delimited by wing veins.

detailed.name: wing crease
detailed.def: The line along which the wing is creased.

detailed.name: wing membrane
detailed.def: The area that is not vein and not sclerotised.

detailed.name: wing region
detailed.def: The area that is delimited by flexion and fold lines on the wing.

detailed.name: wing vein
detailed.def: The area that is linear and sclerotised and acts as support for the wing membrane.

detailed.name: wing venation
detailed.def: The anatomical cluster that is composed of abscissae.

detailed.name: axillular groove
detailed.def: The axillular line that is an impression.

detailed.name: capitate peg sensillum
detailed.def: Mushroom-shaped sensory structure on the flagellum, particularly theclava; sometimes called the sensilla ampullacea.

detailed.name: head muscle
detailed.def: The muscle that is located in the head.

detailed.name: gaine
detailed.def: The anatomical cluster composed of the second valvifer and the third valvula.

detailed.name: gorgeret
detailed.def: The anatomical cluster composed of the second valvifer, second valvula, and third valvula.

detailed.name: lanze
detailed.def: The anatomical cluster that is composed of the first valvifer and the first valvula.

detailed.name: olistheter
detailed.def: The anatomical cluster that is composed of the rhachis of the second valvula and the aulax of the first valvula.

detailed.name: postgenal ridge
detailed.def: The ridge that extends ventrally of the posterior tentorial pit and that is continuous with the tentorium.

detailed.name: profurcal muscle
detailed.def: The thoracic muscle that inserts on the profurca.

detailed.name: probasitarsus
detailed.def: The basitarsus that is located on the protarsus.

key: ventral ramus of the second valvula

detailed.name: ventral ramus of the second valvula
detailed.def: The area of the second valvifer-second vavula-third valvifer complex that bears the rhachis.

detailed.name: pharyngeal muscle
detailed.def: The head muscle that inserts on the pharynx.

detailed.name: postoccipital muscle
detailed.def: The head muscle that inserts on the postocciput.

detailed.name: salivarial muscle
detailed.def: The head muscle that inserts on the dorsal wall of the salivarium or the salivarial sclerite.

detailed.name: mesotrochanteral muscle
detailed.def: The leg muscle that inserts on the mesotrochanter.

detailed.name: hypopharyngeal muscle
detailed.def: The head muscle that inserts on the hypopharyngeal wall.

detailed.name: procoxal muscle
detailed.def: The leg muscle that inserts on the procoxa.

detailed.name: maxillar muscle
detailed.def: The head muscle that inserts on the maxilla.

detailed.name: anterior thoracic spiracle occlusor muscle
detailed.def: The thoracic muscle that inserts on the anterior thoracic spiracle.

detailed.name: laterocervical muscle
